title: First Impressions
label: Black Swan
description: First Impressions (1921) captures Fletcher Henderson exploring early jazz territory on the Black Swan label. The date includes "Sing You Sinners" and "Hop Off". The album comes from the formative years when jazz coalesced from ragtime, blues, and marching band traditions.
significance: The date includes "Sing You Sinners" and "Hop Off".

title: Hot Fives & Sevens
label: Okeh
description: Hot Fives & Sevens is a 2000 box set collection of recordings made by American jazz trumpeter and singer Louis Armstrong with his Hot Five, Hot Seven, and other groups between 1925 and 1930. First released on JSP Records on 22 August 2000, the set was subsequently reissued on Definitive in 2001. A four-disc compilation, the set has received a "crown" as an author's pick in The Penguin Guide to Jazz and is also included in the book's "core collection" recommended for jazz fans.
significance: These recordings established the vocabulary of jazz improvisation. Armstrong's virtuosity and creativity set the template for every jazz soloist who followed.

title: Bix Beiderbecke and the Chicago Cornets
label: Unknown
description: Bix Beiderbecke and the Chicago Cornets is a contemporary jazz album by Bix Beiderbecke from 1926. The album comes from the formative years when jazz coalesced from ragtime, blues, and marching band traditions.
significance: Beiderbecke was one of the most influential jazz soloists of the 1920s, a cornet player noted for an inventive lyrical approach and purity of tone, with such clarity of sound that one contemporary famously described it like "shooting bullets at a bel.

title: Singin' the Blues
label: Unknown
description: "Singing the Blues" is a popular song composed by Melvin Endsley and published in 1956. The highest-charting version was by Guy Mitchell and the first recording of the song was by Marty Robbins. It is not related to the 1920 jazz song "Singin' the Blues" recorded by Frank Trumbauer and Bix Beiderbecke in 1927.
significance: Beiderbecke was one of the most influential jazz soloists of the 1920s, a cornet player noted for an inventive lyrical approach and purity of tone, with such clarity of sound that one contemporary famously described it like "shooting bullets at a bel.

title: Ain't Misbehavin'
label: Unknown
description: "Ain't Misbehavin'" is a 1929 stride jazz/early swing song. Andy Razaf wrote the lyrics to a score by Thomas "Fats" Waller and Harry Brooks for the Broadway musical comedy play Connie's Hot Chocolates. It was published by Mills Music.
significance: Andy Razaf wrote the lyrics to a score by Thomas "Fats" Waller and Harry Brooks for the Broadway musical comedy play Connie's Hot Chocolates.

title: Quintette du Hot Club de France
label: Unknown
description: The Quintette du Hot Club de France, often abbreviated "QdHCdF" or "QHCF", was a jazz group founded in France in 1934 by the guitarist Django Reinhardt and the violinist Stéphane Grappelli. It was active in one form or another until 1948.
significance: It was active in one form or another until 1948.

title: Rhythm Is Our Business
label: Unknown
description: Rhythm Is Our Business is a contemporary jazz album by Jimmie Lunceford from 1934. The album comes from the big band era when jazz was America's popular music, driven by dancing and radio.
significance: A contemporary jazz entry from 1934 by Jimmie Lunceford, documenting the breadth of the swing period.

title: For Dancers Only
label: Unknown
description: With For Dancers Only (1937), bandleader, alto saxophone player Jimmie Lunceford delivers a contemporary jazz program.
significance: A contemporary jazz entry from 1937 by Jimmie Lunceford, documenting the breadth of the swing period.

title: The Joint Is Jumpin'
label: Unknown
description: A Handful of Keys is an album of live performances by jazz pianist Fats Waller & His Rhythm, the only authorized live performances of the musician. It features previously unreleased live radio transcriptions originally broadcast in 1938.
significance: It features previously unreleased live radio transcriptions originally broadcast in 1938.

title: Jack Teagarden's Big Eight / Pee Wee Russell's Rhythmakers
label: Unknown
description: Jack Teagarden's Big Eight / Pee Wee Russell's Rhythmakers is a contemporary jazz album by Jack Teagarden from 1938.
significance: A contemporary jazz entry from 1938 by Jack Teagarden, documenting the breadth of the swing period.

title: Lunceford Special
label: Unknown
description: Bandleader, alto saxophone player Jimmie Lunceford's 1940 album Lunceford Special draws from the contemporary jazz tradition. The album comes from the big band era when jazz was America's popular music, driven by dancing and radio.
significance: A contemporary jazz entry from 1940 by Jimmie Lunceford, documenting the breadth of the swing period.

title: Nuages
label: Unknown
description: "Nuages" is one of the best-known compositions by Django Reinhardt. He recorded at least thirteen versions of the tune, which is a jazz standard and a mainstay of the gypsy swing repertoire. English and French lyrics have been added to the piece, which was originally an instrumental work. The title translated into English is "Clouds", but the adaptation with English lyrics is titled "It's the Bluest Kind of Blues".
significance: He recorded at least thirteen versions of the tune, which is a jazz standard and a mainstay of the gypsy swing repertoire.

title: The Zodiac Suite
label: Asch
description: Zodiac Suite is a series of 12 pieces of jazz music written by the American jazz pianist and composer Mary Lou Williams and first performed in 1945. The suite makes use of elements of classical music alongside jazz, and Williams was influenced by modernism when writing and arranging it. Each song in the suite is inspired by an astrological sign and musicians or performers who were born under it. Williams began writing music for Zodiac Suite in 1942 and finished the composition in 1945.
significance: One of the first extended jazz compositions. Williams bridged the swing era and bebop, earning respect from both camps while charting her own path.

title: Djangology
label: Vogue
description: Jean Reinhardt, known by his Romani nickname Django, was a Belgian-born Romani jazz guitarist and composer who lived most of his life in France. He was one of the first major jazz talents to emerge in Europe and has been hailed as one of its most significant exponents.
significance: Invented European jazz. Django's influence extends from jazz to rock to countless guitarists who marvel at his impossible virtuosity.

title: Genius of Modern Music, Volume 1
label: Blue Note
description: Genius of Modern Music, Vols. One & Two are a pair of separate but related 12" compilation albums (LPs) by American jazz pianist Thelonious Monk released on Blue Note in 1956. Later re-issues on CD include more material recorded at the same sessions but not released on the original LPs.
significance: One & Two are a pair of separate but related 12" compilation albums (LPs) by American jazz pianist Thelonious Monk released on Blue Note in 1956.

title: Lover Man
label: Decca Records
description: "Lover Man " is a 1941 popular song written by Jimmy Davis, Roger ("Ram") Ramirez, and James Sherman. It is particularly associated with Billie Holiday, for whom it was written, and her version was inducted into the Grammy Hall of Fame in 1989.
significance: It is particularly associated with Billie Holiday, for whom it was written, and her version was inducted into the Grammy Hall of Fame in 1989.
keyTracks: Lover Man (Oh, Where Can You Be?)

title: Masterpieces by Ellington
label: Unknown
description: Masterpieces by Ellington is the first LP album by American pianist, composer, and bandleader Duke Ellington, recorded for the Columbia Records in 1950. It was one of the earliest 12-inch LPs to take advantage of the extended time available and consisted of four tracks, three of them "concert arrangements" of Ellington standards and one, "The Tattooed Bride", a recent tone poem.
significance: It was one of the earliest 12-inch LPs to take advantage of the extended time available and consisted of four tracks, three of them "concert arrangements" of Ellington standards and one, "The Tattooed Bride", a recent tone poem.

title: The Amazing Bud Powell, Vol. 1
label: Blue Note
description: The Amazing Bud Powell is a ten-inch LP by American jazz pianist Bud Powell, recorded on August 8, 1949, and May 1, 1951, and released on Blue Note in April 1952. In the first session, Powell performed in quintet with Fats Navarro, Sonny Rollins, Tommy Potter and Roy Haynes, and in trio with Potter and Haynes. In the second, Powell performed in trio with Curley Russell and Max Roach, and solo.
significance: Established the template for bebop piano. Powell's right-hand lines and left-hand comping influenced every jazz pianist who followed.

title: Ambiance Bechet
label: Disques Vogue
description: Recorded for Disques Vogue in 1952, Ambiance Bechet presents clarinetist Sidney Bechet working in a dixieland vein. Among the tracks are "That Old Black Magic" and "Because of You (Sans ton Amour)".
significance: Among the tracks are "That Old Black Magic" and "Because of You (Sans ton Amour)".
keyTracks: I Get a Kick Out of You

title: Bird and Diz
label: Blue Star
description: Bird and Diz is a studio album by jazz saxophonist Charlie Parker and trumpeter Dizzy Gillespie. It was recorded primarily on June 6, 1950, in New York City. Two tracks featured on the original pressing, "Passport" and "Visa", were recorded by Parker, without Gillespie and with different personnel than the other tracks, in March and May 1949. The album was originally issued in 1952 in 10" format as a collection of 78 rpm singles on the Verve subsidiary label Clef Records.
significance: It was recorded primarily on June 6, 1950, in New York City.

title: Bird and Diz
label: Blue Star
description: Bird and Diz is a studio album by jazz saxophonist Charlie Parker and trumpeter Dizzy Gillespie. It was recorded primarily on June 6, 1950, in New York City. Two tracks featured on the original pressing, "Passport" and "Visa", were recorded by Parker, without Gillespie and with different personnel than the other tracks, in March and May 1949. The album was originally issued in 1952 in 10" format as a collection of 78 rpm singles on the Verve subsidiary label Clef Records.
significance: It was recorded primarily on June 6, 1950, in New York City.

title: On Parade
label: Disques Vogue
description: A early jazz outing from 1952, On Parade features clarinetist Sidney Bechet recording for Disques Vogue. Among the tracks are "Le marchand de poissons" and "Si tu vois ma mère". The album comes from the formative years when jazz coalesced from ragtime, blues, and marching band traditions.
significance: He was one of the first important soloists in jazz, and first recorded several months before trumpeter Louis Armstrong.

title: Solo Flight
label: Columbia
description: With Solo Flight (1952), pianist Erroll Garner delivers a bebop program for the Columbia label. Standouts include "Cocktails for Two" and "It Don't Mean a Thing (If It Ain't Got That Swing)". Columbia Records gave jazz wide exposure, releasing landmark albums by Miles Davis, Thelonious Monk, and Dave Brubeck.
significance: It was first recorded in 1956 with Mitch Miller and his orchestra, and played a prominent part in the 1971 motion picture Play Misty for Me.
keyTracks: It Don't Mean a Thing (If It Ain't Got That Swing)

title: Holiday in Piano
label: Mercury Records
description: With Holiday in Piano (1953), pianist Lennie Tristano delivers a bebop program for the Mercury Records label. Among the tracks are "Can't Get Started With You" and "Out On A Limb".
significance: Among the tracks are "Can't Get Started With You" and "Out On A Limb".

title: Jazz at Massey Hall
label: Debut
description: Jazz at Massey Hall is a live album released in December 1953 by jazz combo The Quintet through Debut Records. It was recorded on 15 May 1953 at Massey Hall in Toronto, Canada. Credited to "the Quintet", the jazz group was composed of five leading "modern" players of the day: Dizzy Gillespie, Charlie Parker, Bud Powell, Charles Mingus, and Max Roach. It was the only time that the five musicians recorded together as a unit, and it was the last recorded meeting of Parker and Gillespie.
significance: Documents the bebop era's all-star summit. The concert has become legendary as the greatest bebop performance ever recorded.

title: King of the Tenors
label: Verve
description: King of the Tenors is an album by American jazz saxophonist Ben Webster featuring tracks recorded in 1953 for the Norgran label and originally released as The Consummate Artistry of Ben Webster. The album was re-issued in 1957 on Verve Records as King of the Tenors, and has been released with that title ever since. Webster is accompanied by the Oscar Peterson Trio, and, on several tracks, by Benny Carter and Harry "Sweets" Edison.
significance: The album was re-issued in 1957 on Verve Records as King of the Tenors, and has been released with that title ever since.

title: Oscar Peterson Plays George Gershwin
label: Clef Records
description: Night Train is an album by the Oscar Peterson Trio, released in 1963 by Verve Records. The album includes jazz, blues and R&B standards, as well as "Hymn to Freedom", one of Peterson's best known original compositions.
significance: As a virtuoso who is considered to be one of the greatest jazz pianists of all time, Peterson released more than 200 recordings, won eight Grammy Awards, as well as a lifetime achievement award from the Recording Academy, and received numerous other .

title: The Eminent Jay Jay Johnson, Volume 1
label: Unknown
description: The Eminent Jay Jay Johnson, Vols. 1 & 2 are a pair of separate but related compilation albums by American jazz trombonist J. J. Johnson, recorded over three sessions between 1953 and 1955 and released on Blue Note in 1956.
significance: 1 & 2 are a pair of separate but related compilation albums by American jazz trombonist J.

title: The Great Artistry of Django Reinhardt
label: Clef Records
description: Jean Reinhardt, known by his Romani nickname Django, was a Belgian-born Romani jazz guitarist and composer who lived most of his life in France. He was one of the first major jazz talents to emerge in Europe and has been hailed as one of its most significant exponents.
significance: He was one of the first major jazz talents to emerge in Europe and has been hailed as one of its most significant exponents.

title: A Night at Birdland
label: Blue Note
description: A Night at Birdland, Vols. 1 & 2 are a pair of separate but related live albums by the Art Blakey Quintet. They were recorded at the Birdland jazz club on February 21, 1954 and released on Blue Note in 1956. The performance was originally spread out over three 10" LPs as A Night at Birdland Vols. 1–3 (1954).
significance: One of the first great hard bop recordings, establishing the template for the Jazz Messengers and countless groups to follow.

title: Chet Baker Sings
label: Pacific Jazz
description: Chet Baker Sings is the debut vocal album by jazz musician Chet Baker, released in 1954 by Pacific Jazz Records. In 2001, the album received the Grammy Hall of Fame Award. Baker would return to selections from this album throughout his career. "My Funny Valentine" was regularly included in his concert performances and is considered by some to be his signature song.
significance: Proved that jazz vocals could be introverted and intimate. Baker's romantic fragility influenced generations of singers.
keyTracks: I Fall in Love Too Easily

title: Lester Young with The Oscar Peterson Trio #1
label: Blue Star
description: Lester Young with the Oscar Peterson Trio is a 1954 studio album by Lester Young, accompanied by Oscar Peterson's working trio of the time, plus drummer J. C. Heard. The music on this album was originally released as three separate albums: Lester Young with the Oscar Peterson Trio #1 and Lester Young with the Oscar Peterson Trio #2, both released in June 1954, and The President. It was collated for this 1997 reissue by Verve Records.
significance: The music on this album was originally released as three separate albums: Lester Young with the Oscar Peterson Trio #1 and Lester Young with the Oscar Peterson Trio #2, both released in June 1954, and The President.

title: Lester Young with the Oscar Peterson Trio #2
label: Norgran Records
description: Lester Young with the Oscar Peterson Trio is a 1954 studio album by Lester Young, accompanied by Oscar Peterson's working trio of the time, plus drummer J. C. Heard. The music on this album was originally released as three separate albums: Lester Young with the Oscar Peterson Trio #1 and Lester Young with the Oscar Peterson Trio #2, both released in June 1954, and The President. It was collated for this 1997 reissue by Verve Records.
significance: The music on this album was originally released as three separate albums: Lester Young with the Oscar Peterson Trio #1 and Lester Young with the Oscar Peterson Trio #2, both released in June 1954, and The President.
keyTracks: There Will Never Be Another You
On the Sunny Side of the Street

title: Louis Armstrong Plays W.C. Handy
label: Columbia
description: Louis Armstrong Plays W. C. Handy is a 1954 studio release by Louis Armstrong and His All Stars, described by Allmusic as "Louis Armstrong's finest record of the 1950s" and "essential music for all serious jazz collections". Columbia CD released the album on CD in 1986 in a much altered form, with alternative versions in place of many of the original songs, but restored the original with its 1997 re-issue, which also included additional tracks: a brief interview by the producer, George Avakian, with W. C. Handy; a joke told by Louis Armstrong; and several rehearsal versions of the songs.
significance: Handy is a 1954 studio release by Louis Armstrong and His All Stars, described by Allmusic as "Louis Armstrong's finest record of the 1950s" and "essential music for all serious jazz collections".

title: Tatum, Carter, Bellson
label: Clef Records
description: Pianist Art Tatum recorded commercially from 1932 until near his death. He recorded nearly 400 titles, if airchecks and informal, private recordings are included. He recorded for Brunswick (1933), Decca (1934–41), Capitol and for the labels associated with Norman Granz (1953–56).
significance: He recorded nearly 400 titles, if airchecks and informal, private recordings are included.

title: The President
label: Norgran Records
description: Lester Willis Young, nicknamed "Pres" or "Prez", was an American jazz tenor saxophonist and occasional clarinetist.
significance: A Norgran Records session that documents clarinetist Lester Young working through the bebop vocabulary of the bebop period.
keyTracks: I Can’t Give You Anything but Love

title: Horace Silver and the Jazz Messengers
label: Blue Note
description: Horace Silver and the Jazz Messengers is an album by Horace Silver and the Jazz Messengers compiling two 1955 10" LPs—Horace Silver Quintet, Vol. 3 and Horace Silver Quintet, Vol. 4 —recorded on November 13, 1954, and February 6, 1955, respectively, and released on Blue Note in October 1956—Silver’s debut 12". The quintet features horn section Hank Mobley and Kenny Dorham and rhythm section Doug Watkins and Art Blakey.
significance: 4 —recorded on November 13, 1954, and February 6, 1955, respectively, and released on Blue Note in October 1956—Silver’s debut 12".

title: In the Land of Hi-Fi
label: EmArcy
description: In the Land of Hi-Fi is a 1955 studio album by the American jazz singer Sarah Vaughan. It was recorded October 25–27, 1955, in New York City. Alternate takes from these sessions, featuring a young Cannonball Adderley, were compiled on The Complete Sarah Vaughan On Mercury, Vol. 1 - Great Jazz Years 1954-1956.
significance: It was recorded October 25–27, 1955, in New York City.

title: Sarah Vaughan with Clifford Brown
label: EmArcy
description: Sarah Vaughan, reissued in 1991 as Sarah Vaughan with Clifford Brown, is a 1955 jazz album featuring singer Sarah Vaughan and trumpeter Clifford Brown, released on the EmArcy label. It was the only collaboration between the two musicians. Well received, though not without some criticism, the album was Vaughan's own favorite among her works through 1980. The album was inducted into the Grammy Hall of Fame in 1999.
significance: Proved that Vaughan could hold her own with the finest instrumentalists. The chemistry between voice and trumpet is magical.

title: Study in Brown
label: EmArcy
description: Study in Brown is a Clifford Brown and Max Roach album. The album consists predominantly of originals by members of the band. The songs "Lands End", by tenor saxophonist Harold Land, and "Sandu", by Brown, have gone on to become jazz standards. The song "George's Dilemma" is also known as "Ulcer Department". Brown's solo on "Cherokee" is among the most acclaimed solos in jazz.
significance: Established Brown as the most promising trumpeter of his generation before his tragic death. His influence lived on through Lee Morgan, Freddie Hubbard, and others.

title: Telefunken Blues
label: Savoy Records
description: Telefunken Blues is an album led by jazz drummer Kenny Clarke recorded in late 1954 and early 1955 and first released on the Savoy label.
significance: Released by Savoy Records during the bebop revolution, when small groups pushed jazz toward harmonic complexity and virtuosity, this set captures Kenny Clarke in characteristic form.

title: The Eminent Jay Jay Johnson, Volume 2
label: Unknown
description: The Eminent Jay Jay Johnson, Vols. 1 & 2 are a pair of separate but related compilation albums by American jazz trombonist J. J. Johnson, recorded over three sessions between 1953 and 1955 and released on Blue Note in 1956.
significance: 1 & 2 are a pair of separate but related compilation albums by American jazz trombonist J.

title: The Genius of Art Tatum #9
label: Clef Records
description: The Genius of Art Tatum is a 1953–54 series of solo albums by jazz pianist Art Tatum originally issued on LP over 11 volumes. First released on the Clef Records label, they were added to the Grammy Hall of Fame in 1978. A 7-CD box-set of these recordings, now under the title of The Complete Pablo Solo Masterpieces, was issued on the Pablo label in July 1991.
significance: First released on the Clef Records label, they were added to the Grammy Hall of Fame in 1978.
keyTracks: I'll See You in My Dreams
Would You Lie to Take a Walk
I've Got a Crush on You

title: Ellington at Newport
label: Columbia
description: Ellington at Newport is a 1956 live jazz album by Duke Ellington and his band of their 1956 concert at the Newport Jazz Festival, a concert which revitalized Ellington's flagging career. Jazz promoter George Wein describes the 1956 concert as "the greatest performance of [Ellington's] career... It stood for everything that jazz had been and could be." It is included in the book 1001 Albums You Must Hear Before You Die, which ranks it "one of the most famous... in jazz history". The original release was partly recreated in the studio after the Ellington Orchestra's festival appearance.
significance: This concert marked Ellington's triumphant comeback and proved big band jazz still had the power to move audiences in the bebop era.

title: Kenny Burrell
label: Unknown
description: Kenny Burrell is an album by guitarist Kenny Burrell recorded in 1957 and released on the Prestige label.
significance: Guitarist Kenny Burrell's hard bop sensibility is on display in this 1956 session, a document of the hard bop era.

title: Pithecanthropus Erectus
label: Atlantic
description: Pithecanthropus Erectus is a studio album by jazz composer and bassist Charles Mingus. It was released in August 1956 through Atlantic Records. Mingus noted that this was the first album where he taught arrangements to his musicians by ear instead of putting the chords and arrangements in writing.
significance: Mingus noted that this was the first album where he taught arrangements to his musicians by ear instead of putting the chords and arrangements in writing.

title: Saxophone Colossus
label: Prestige
description: Saxophone Colossus is the sixth studio album by American jazz saxophonist Sonny Rollins. Perhaps Rollins's best-known album, it is often considered his breakthrough record. It was recorded monophonically on June 22, 1956, with producer Bob Weinstock and engineer Rudy Van Gelder at the latter's studio in Hackensack, New Jersey. Rollins led a quartet on the album that included pianist Tommy Flanagan, bassist Doug Watkins, and drummer Max Roach.
significance: Demonstrated that hard bop could be both intellectually rigorous and emotionally accessible. Rollins's thematic improvisation influenced generations of saxophonists.
keyTracks: You Don't Know What Love Is

title: The Benny Goodman Story, Vol. 2
label: Decca Records
description: The Benny Goodman Story, Vol. 2 documents a 1956 studio date for Decca Records, with Benny Goodman leading a tight big band ensemble. Highlights include 'Memories of You' and 'China Boy'.
significance: 2 documents a 1956 studio date for Decca Records, with Benny Goodman leading a tight big band ensemble.

title: The Trio, Volume 3: Everybody Likes Hampton Hawes
label: Contemporary Records
description: The Sermon is an album of spirituals by American jazz pianist Hampton Hawes recorded in 1958, but not released on the Contemporary label until 1987.
significance: A Contemporary Records session that documents pianist Hampton Hawes working through the cool jazz vocabulary of the hard bop period.

title: 2 Guitars
label: Prestige
description: 2 Guitars is an album by guitarists Kenny Burrell and Jimmy Raney recorded in 1957 and released on the Prestige label.
significance: Part of Prestige's hard bop catalog. Prestige under Bob Weinstock was known for its prolific output of blowing sessions, often recording a full album in a single day.

title: At Carnegie Hall
artist: Thelonious Monk Quartet with John Coltrane
label: Blue Note
description: Thelonious Monk Quartet with John Coltrane at Carnegie Hall is a live album by the Thelonious Monk Quartet, which included John Coltrane at the time. It was recorded at Carnegie Hall on November 29, 1957, and was released on September 27, 2005, by Blue Note.
significance: Documents the brief but legendary collaboration between Monk and Coltrane, two of jazz's most distinctive voices.

title: Birth of the Cool
label: Capitol
description: Birth of the Cool is a compilation album by the American jazz trumpeter and bandleader Miles Davis. It was released in February or March 1957 on Capitol Records. It compiles eleven tracks recorded by Davis's nonet for the label over the course of three sessions during 1949 and 1950.
significance: Named and defined the cool jazz movement. Its influence on West Coast jazz and the Third Stream movement was immense.

title: Blues and Vanilla
label: RCA Victor
description: Blues and Vanilla is an album by saxophonist Jack Montrose's Quintet with Red Norvo recorded in 1956 for the RCA Victor label.
significance: Released by RCA Victor during the cool jazz movement, favoring lighter timbres, relaxed tempos, and a chamber sensibility, this set captures Red Norvo in characteristic form.

title: Brilliant Corners
label: Riverside
description: A challenging album that showcases Monk's unique compositional vision, recorded in 1956 with an all-star group including Sonny Rollins and Max Roach. The title track was so rhythmically and melodically complex that after 25 takes, no complete version existed—the final version was spliced together from multiple attempts. The session featured unusual instrumentation including celeste, adding to Monk's distinctive sound. 'Pannonica' is a beautiful ballad dedicated to his patron, the Baroness Pannonica de Koenigswarter.
significance: Demonstrates Monk's uncompromising vision and the extraordinary challenge of playing his angular, unpredictable compositions. The album established him as one of jazz's most important composers and earned him wider recognition after years of being dismissed as eccentric. It helped launch Monk's commercial breakthrough period that culminated in his Time magazine cover.

reviews.quote: Monk's singular genius is fully on display, with compositions that challenge and reward in equal measure.

title: Dizzy Atmosphere
label: Specialty Records
description: Dizzy Atmosphere is an album featuring members of Dizzy Gillespie's Orchestra including trombonist Al Grey, saxophonist Billy Mitchell and trumpeter Lee Morgan recorded in 1957 and released on the Specialty label.
significance: A hard bop entry from 1957 by Lee Morgan, documenting the breadth of the hard bop period.

title: Ella and Her Fellas
label: Decca Records
description: Like Someone in Love is a 1957 studio album by the American jazz singer Ella Fitzgerald, with a studio orchestra arranged and conducted by Frank DeVol. This album represents a fine example of Ella's singing from this period, recorded at the same time as her albums with Louis Armstrong.
significance: This album represents a fine example of Ella's singing from this period, recorded at the same time as her albums with Louis Armstrong.
keyTracks: You Won’t Be Satisfied (Until You Break My Heart)
Stone Cold Dead in the Market (He Had It Coming)
I Gotta Have My Baby Back

title: Four Trombones
label: Debut
description: Mingus is an album by the jazz bassist and composer Charles Mingus. The album was recorded in October and November 1960 in New York and released in late 1961 on Nat Hentoff's Candid label.
significance: The album was recorded in October and November 1960 in New York and released in late 1961 on Nat Hentoff's Candid label.

title: Jay and Kai
label: Unknown
description: Jay and Kai is an album by American jazz trombonists J. J. Johnson and Kai Winding featuring performances recorded in 1955 and 1956 for the Columbia label.
significance: Johnson and Kai Winding featuring performances recorded in 1955 and 1956 for the Columbia label.

title: Jazz by Sun Ra
label: Transition
description: Jazz By Sun Ra is the debut album by Sun Ra. The record label for the first pressing says "07-12-56", presumably when it was recorded. The LP originally appeared on Tom Wilson's short-lived Transition Records. In the mid-1960s it was purchased by Delmark Records owner Bob Koester, finally being reissued in 1967.
significance: The record label for the first pressing says "07-12-56", presumably when it was recorded.

title: Louis Armstrong Meets Oscar Peterson
label: Verve
description: Louis Armstrong Meets Oscar Peterson is a 1959 studio album by Louis Armstrong, accompanied by Oscar Peterson.
significance: This Verve date features "That Old Feeling" as part of Louis Armstrong's vocal jazz output from 1957.
keyTracks: How Long Has This Been Going On?

title: Mulligan Meets Monk
label: Riverside
description: Mulligan Meets Monk is a studio album by American jazz pianist Thelonious Monk and baritone saxophonist Gerry Mulligan, originally released on Riverside Records in 1957. It has been reissued numerous times. It was remastered for CD in 1987 with three additional alternative takes from the original session.
significance: It was remastered for CD in 1987 with three additional alternative takes from the original session.

title: Mulligan Meets Monk
label: Riverside
description: Mulligan Meets Monk is a studio album by American jazz pianist Thelonious Monk and baritone saxophonist Gerry Mulligan, originally released on Riverside Records in 1957. It has been reissued numerous times. It was remastered for CD in 1987 with three additional alternative takes from the original session.
significance: It was remastered for CD in 1987 with three additional alternative takes from the original session.

title: Sonny Rollins, Volume 2
label: Blue Note
description: On Sonny Rollins, Volume 2 (1957), Sonny Rollins delivers a focused exploration of hard bop, recorded for Blue Note. Among its key performances are 'Why Don't I' and 'Wail March'.
significance: Among its key performances are 'Why Don't I' and 'Wail March'.
keyTracks: You Stepped Out of a Dream

title: Such Sweet Thunder
label: Unknown
description: Such Sweet Thunder is a Duke Ellington album, released in 1957. The record is a twelve-part suite based on the work of William Shakespeare.
significance: The record is a twelve-part suite based on the work of William Shakespeare.

title: The Clown
label: Atlantic
description: The Clown is a studio album by the jazz bassist and composer Charles Mingus. It was released in September 1957 through Atlantic Records. The recording took place on February 13 and March 12, 1957. The title track features the improvised narration of Jean Shepherd.
significance: It was released in September 1957 through Atlantic Records.

title: The Most Happy Piano
label: Columbia
description: With The Most Happy Piano (1957), pianist Erroll Garner delivers a cool jazz program for the Columbia label. Among the tracks are "Girl of My Dreams" and "But Not for Me".
significance: It was first recorded in 1956 with Mitch Miller and his orchestra, and played a prominent part in the 1971 motion picture Play Misty for Me.

title: Thelonious Himself
label: Riverside
description: Thelonious Himself is a studio album by Thelonious Monk released in 1957 by Riverside Records. It was Monk's fourth album for the label. The album features Monk playing solo piano, except for the final track, "Monk's Mood", which features John Coltrane on tenor saxophone and Wilbur Ware on bass. It was Monk's second solo piano studio album, and it was the first made by an American label and distributed in the United States.
significance: It was Monk's second solo piano studio album, and it was the first made by an American label and distributed in the United States.

title: ’Round About Midnight
label: Columbia
description: A 1957 Columbia session, ’Round About Midnight finds Miles Davis pushing deeper into hard bop territory. The session exudes the understated elegance and sophisticated lyricism of the cool jazz school. Among its key performances are ''Round Midnight' and 'Ah-Leu-Cha'.
significance: The session exudes the understated elegance and sophisticated lyricism of the cool jazz school.

title: At the Pershing: But Not for Me
label: Argo
description: At the Pershing: But Not for Me is a 1958 jazz live album by pianist Ahmad Jamal. According to the album jacket, the tapes were made on January 16, 1958, at the Pershing Lounge of Chicago's Pershing Hotel and each set played that night was recorded, a total of 43 tracks, of which 8 were selected by Jamal for the album. The LP was released as Argo Records LP-628. Jamal's previous releases on Argo had been from previously made masters; this was his first release recorded for Argo, and his first album recorded live.
significance: Influenced Miles Davis's conception of space in music. Jamal's light touch and dramatic pauses changed jazz piano.
keyTracks: Surrey with the Fringe on Top

title: Blue Train
label: Blue Note
description: Coltrane's only album as a leader for Blue Note Records, recorded on September 15, 1957, at Rudy Van Gelder's studio in Hackensack, New Jersey. The all-star sextet featured Lee Morgan on trumpet, Curtis Fuller on trombone, Kenny Drew on piano, Paul Chambers on bass, and Philly Joe Jones on drums. The title track became one of Coltrane's signature tunes, with its memorable melody and his powerful, driving solo. 'Moment's Notice' showcases his rapid chord-change improvisation that would later evolve into 'Giant Steps.'
significance: A masterpiece of hard bop that demonstrates Coltrane's growing command as a bandleader and composer. It bridges the gap between hard bop and his later modal explorations, featuring the complex harmonic substitutions that would become his trademark 'Coltrane changes.' The iconic cover photo by Francis Wolff became one of the most recognizable images in jazz.

reviews.quote: Blue Train showcases Coltrane at his most commanding, leading an all-star group through some of his finest compositions.
reviews.author: Stephen Thomas Erlewine

title: Branching Out
label: Riverside
description: Branching Out is an album by jazz cornetist Nat Adderley, released on the Riverside label and featuring performances by Adderley with Johnny Griffin, and the Three Sounds.
significance: He was the younger brother of saxophonist Julian "Cannonball" Adderley, with whom he played for many years.

title: Carmen for Cool Ones
label: Decca Records
description: Carmen for Cool Ones is a 1958 album by jazz singer Carmen McRae, arranged and directed by cellist Fred Katz.
significance: She is considered one of the most influential jazz vocalists of the 20th century and is remembered for her behind-the-beat phrasing and ironic interpretation of lyrics.

title: Ella Fitzgerald Sings the Irving Berlin Song Book
label: His Master’s Voice
description: Ella Fitzgerald Sings the Irving Berlin Song Book is a 1958 studio album by the American jazz singer Ella Fitzgerald, with a studio orchestra conducted and arranged by Paul Weston, focusing on the songs of Irving Berlin. It was part of the popular and influential Songbook series.
significance: It was part of the popular and influential Songbook series.
keyTracks: Let’s Face the Music and Dance

title: Ellington Indigos (Expanded Edition)
label: Columbia
description: Ellington Indigos is a 1958 studio album by Duke Ellington and His Orchestra, released on Columbia Records. The program collects some of Ellington's most lyrical compositions and arrangements, including definitive readings of "Solitude," "Mood Indigo," and "Autumn Leaves," all rendered in the rich, burnished tones of the Ellington orchestra.
significance: This Columbia date features "Solitude" as part of Duke Ellington's swing output from 1958.
keyTracks: The Sky Fell Down - Mono Version

title: Going for Myself
label: Verve
description: Going For Myself is an album by Harry "Sweets" Edison and Lester Young, accompanied by Oscar Peterson. The album is one of Young's last studio recordings.
significance: The album is one of Young's last studio recordings.

title: Lady in Satin
label: Columbia
description: Lady in Satin is an album by the jazz singer Billie Holiday, released in 1958 on Columbia Records, catalogue CL 1157 in mono and CS 8048 in stereo. It is the penultimate album completed by the singer, and the last to be released in her lifetime. Her final album, Last Recording, was recorded in March 1959, and released just after her death. The original album was produced by Irving Townsend and engineered by Fred Plaut.
significance: Her final album, Last Recording, was recorded in March 1959, and released just after her death.
keyTracks: I'm a Fool to Want You
You Don't Know What Love Is
I Get Along Without You Very Well

title: Latin Kick
label: Fantasy
description: Latin Kick is a latin jazz album by Cal Tjader, released on Fantasy in 1958. The date includes "Invitation" and "Lover, Come Back to Me". The album comes from the hard bop period, reconnecting jazz with blues and gospel while keeping bebop's sophistication.
significance: was an American Latin Jazz musician, often described as the most successful non-Latino Latin musician.

title: Little Girl Blue
label: Bethlehem Records
description: Little Girl Blue: Jazz as Played in an Exclusive Side Street Club is the debut album by the jazz pianist and singer Nina Simone. It was released by Bethlehem Records in February 1959. It was recorded in late 1957 but its release was held up by the label. Due to the delay and the lack of any promotional single either immediately before or alongside the album, Simone was dissatisfied with Bethlehem and signed with Colpix Records in April 1959. She recorded the tracks for her second album – what would become The Amazing Nina Simone – the same month.
significance: It was released by Bethlehem Records in February 1959.

title: Little Niles
label: United Artists
description: Little Niles is an album by American jazz pianist Randy Weston recorded in 1958 and first released on the United Artists label. The album was later released as part of a Blue Note compilation under the same title. All the tracks are Weston originals and, as indicated in the LP's liner notes by Langston Hughes, the album was inspired by Weston's children Niles and Pamela, who are directly referenced in "Little Niles" and "Pam's Waltz" and feature in the cover photograph.
significance: The album was later released as part of a Blue Note compilation under the same title.

title: Lou Takes Off
label: Blue Note
description: Lou Takes Off is an album by American jazz saxophonist Lou Donaldson recorded on December 15, 1957 and released on Blue Note the following year. The sextet features brass section Donald Byrd and Curtis Fuller and rhythm section Sonny Clark, Jamil Nasser and Art Taylor.
significance: The sextet features brass section Donald Byrd and Curtis Fuller and rhythm section Sonny Clark, Jamil Nasser and Art Taylor.

title: Milestones
label: CBS
description: Milestones is a studio album by Miles Davis. It was recorded with his "first great quintet" and released in September 1958 by Columbia Records.
significance: It was recorded with his "first great quintet" and released in September 1958 by Columbia Records.

title: Minor Move
label: Capitol Records
description: Minor Move is an album by American hard bop tenor Tina Brooks. It features performances by Brooks, Lee Morgan, Sonny Clark, Doug Watkins and Art Blakey. It was recorded on March 16, 1958, and was the first album Brooks recorded as a leader for the Blue Note label. The album, however, remained unreleased until being issued in Japan in 1980. In 2000, Minor Move was released on CD. The composition "Nutville" is sometimes credited to Lee Morgan, but as producer Michael Cuscuna explains in the liner notes to the 2000 release: "Lee brought the tune to the session, but never claimed credit for it.
significance: It was recorded on March 16, 1958, and was the first album Brooks recorded as a leader for the Blue Note label.

title: Moanin'
label: Blue Note
description: The definitive hard bop album, recorded on October 30, 1958, at Rudy Van Gelder's studio with the classic Messengers lineup: Lee Morgan on trumpet, Benny Golson on tenor saxophone, Bobby Timmons on piano, and Jymie Merritt on bass. Timmons' iconic gospel-influenced title track opens with one of the most recognizable piano riffs in jazz, immediately establishing the album's blend of church and bop. Golson's sophisticated compositions, including 'Along Came Betty' and 'Blues March,' showcase the band's range from soulful blues to intricate arrangements.
significance: Defined the hard bop sound and became a template for countless jazz albums that followed. The title track's call-and-response structure, derived from Black church music, proved that jazz could be both intellectually rigorous and emotionally direct. Blakey's powerful drumming and Timmons' funky piano established the foundation for soul jazz.

reviews.quote: The quintessential hard bop album, Moanin' defined what the Jazz Messengers stood for and influenced countless musicians.

title: Somethin' Else
label: Blue Note
description: Somethin' Else is an album by American jazz saxophonist Julian "Cannonball" Adderley, recorded on March 9, 1958 and released on Blue Note in August later that year—his only album for the label. Also on the session is trumpeter Miles Davis in one of his handful of recording dates for Blue Note. Adderley was a member of Davis' group at the time, and the album was recorded shortly after Davis' own landmark album Milestones.
significance: A perfect example of hard bop's blend of sophistication and soul. The collaboration between Adderley and Davis was magic.

title: Songs for Distingué Lovers
label: Verve
description: Songs for Distingué Lovers is an album by jazz singer Billie Holiday, released in 1958 on Verve Records. It was originally available in both mono and stereo. It was recorded at Capitol Studios in Los Angeles from January 3 to January 9, 1957, and produced by Norman Granz.
significance: It was recorded at Capitol Studios in Los Angeles from January 3 to January 9, 1957, and produced by Norman Granz.
keyTracks: One for My Baby (and One More for the Road)

title: Sonny Rollins and the Big Brass
label: MetroJazz
description: Sonny Rollins and the Big Brass is an album by jazz saxophonist Sonny Rollins, recorded for the MetroJazz label, later reissued on Verve Records as Sonny Rollins/Brass - Sonny Rollins/Trio.
significance: A MetroJazz session that documents tenor saxophonist Sonny Rollins working through the hard bop vocabulary of the hard bop period.

title: The Atomic Mr. Basie
label: Roulette
description: The Atomic Mr. Basie (originally called Basie, also known as E=MC2 and reissued in 1994 as The Complete Atomic Basie) is a 1958 album by Count Basie, featuring the song arrangements of Neal Hefti and the Count Basie Orchestra. It is included in the book 1001 Albums You Must Hear Before You Die, Will Fulford-Jones calling it "Basie's last great record." It was voted number 411 in the third edition of Colin Larkin's All Time Top 1000 Albums (2000).
significance: Basie (originally called Basie, also known as E=MC2 and reissued in 1994 as The Complete Atomic Basie) is a 1958 album by Count Basie, featuring the song arrangements of Neal Hefti and the Count Basie Orchestra.

title: The Oscar Peterson Trio at Newport
label: Columbia
description: Oscar Peterson and Roy Eldridge is an album by Oscar Peterson, on which he is accompanied by Roy Eldridge.
significance: Trumpeter Roy Eldridge's hard bop sensibility is on display in this 1958 session, a document of the cool jazz era.

title: Abbey Is Blue
label: Riverside
description: Anna Marie Wooldridge, known professionally as Abbey Lincoln, was an American jazz vocalist, songwriter, and actress. She was a civil rights activist beginning in the 1960s. Lincoln made a career out of delivering deeply felt presentations of standards, as well as writing and singing her own material.
significance: Established Lincoln as a serious jazz artist, not just a glamorous singer. Her intense, theatrical approach influenced generations of jazz vocalists.

title: Bags & Flutes
label: Atlantic
description: Bags & Flutes is an album by American jazz vibraphonist Milt Jackson featuring performances recorded in 1957 and released on the Atlantic label.
significance: From the cool jazz movement, favoring lighter timbres, relaxed tempos, and a chamber sensibility, this album positions Milt Jackson within the cool jazz conversation of the time.
keyTracks: I'm Afraid the Masquerade Is Over

title: Ella Fitzgerald Sings the George and Ira Gershwin Song Book
label: Verve
description: Ella Fitzgerald Sings the George and Ira Gershwin Song Book is a box set by American jazz singer Ella Fitzgerald that contains songs by George and Ira Gershwin with arrangements by Nelson Riddle. It was produced by Norman Granz, Fitzgerald's manager and the founder of Verve Records. Fifty-nine songs were recorded in the span of eight months in 1959.
significance: Fifty-nine songs were recorded in the span of eight months in 1959.
keyTracks: Let’s Call the Whole Thing Off

title: Go
label: Vee-Jay Records
description: Go is an album by jazz bassist Paul Chambers recorded in Chicago in 1959 and released by the Vee-Jay label. Featured musicians include trumpeter Freddie Hubbard, alto saxophonist Cannonball Adderley, pianist Wynton Kelly and drummer Philly Joe Jones. The album was re-released in 1997 as a double CD featuring additional recordings and alternate takes from the sessions and included as part of the Mosaic Box Set The Complete Vee Jay Paul Chambers/Wynton Kelly Sessions 1959-61 in 2000.
significance: The album was re-released in 1997 as a double CD featuring additional recordings and alternate takes from the sessions and included as part of the Mosaic Box Set The Complete Vee Jay Paul Chambers/Wynton Kelly Sessions 1959-61 in 2000.

title: Kind of Blue
label: Columbia
description: The best-selling jazz album of all time, Kind of Blue introduced modal jazz to the world. Recorded at Columbia's 30th Street Studio in New York on March 2 and April 22, 1959, the album captures spontaneous creativity at its peak—most tracks were first takes with minimal rehearsal. The legendary sextet featured John Coltrane and Cannonball Adderley on saxophones, pianist Bill Evans (Wynton Kelly on 'Freddie Freeloader'), bassist Paul Chambers, and drummer Jimmy Cobb. Evans contributed the album's modal framework, drawing from impressionist composers like Ravel and his studies with theorist George Russell.
significance: Revolutionized jazz by moving away from bebop's complex chord changes to modal improvisation based on scales, opening new harmonic possibilities that influenced countless musicians across genres from rock to hip-hop. It remains the quintessential introduction to jazz, selling over five million copies and consistently ranking among the greatest albums ever recorded in any genre.

reviews.quote: The greatest jazz album ever made, and one of the most significant records in the history of American music.
reviews.author: Thom Jurek

reviews.quote: Not just one of the greatest jazz albums of all time, but one of the greatest albums of all time, period.

title: Looking Ahead!
label: Contemporary
description: Looking Ahead! is the second studio album by American jazz pianist Cecil Taylor, recorded for Contemporary Records in June 1958 and released the following year. The album features bassist Buell Neidlinger, drummer Denis Charles, and vibraphonist Earl Griffith.
significance: Documents Taylor's style before it became fully abstract. The tension between his radical piano and the walking bass creates fascinating friction.

title: Mingus Ah Um
label: Columbia
description: Mingus Ah Um is a studio album by American jazz musician Charles Mingus which was released in October 1959 by Columbia Records. It was his first album recorded for Columbia. The title is a corruption of an imaginary Latin declension. It is common for Latin students to memorize Latin adjectives by first saying the masculine nominative, then the feminine nominative, and finally the neuter nominative singular —implying a transformation of his name, Mingus, Minga, Mingum. The album was inducted into the Grammy Hall of Fame in 2013. It was ranked 380 on the Rolling Stone's 500 Greatest Albums of All Time. The cover features a painting by S.
significance: Showcased Mingus as one of jazz's greatest composers and bandleaders. 'Goodbye Pork Pie Hat' became one of the most beloved jazz elegies.
keyTracks: Better Git It in Your Soul

title: Nina Simone at Town Hall
label: Colpix
description: Nina Simone at Town Hall is the first live album by Nina Simone, released in December 1959. It was her third album of that year, her second album for Colpix Records, and her first live album. The basis for the record was Simone's performance at the Town Hall, New York, on 12 September 1959. All of the songs performed at the concert are on the album; however, three of the tracks are studio versions cut the following month.
significance: Established Simone as a unique voice—neither jazz nor classical nor folk, but all three fused into something new and powerful.
keyTracks: Black Is the Color of My True Love's Hair

title: Sonny Side Up
label: Columbia Records
description: Sonny Side Up is an album by trumpeter Dizzy Gillespie, and the tenor saxophonists Sonny Stitt and Sonny Rollins, recorded in December 1957 in New York City. It was released in 1959 on producer Norman Granz's newly launched Verve label. Pianist Ray Bryant, bassist Tommy Bryant, and drummer Charlie Persip form the rhythm section.
significance: It was released in 1959 on producer Norman Granz's newly launched Verve label.
keyTracks: On the Sunny Side of the Street

title: The Shape of Jazz to Come
label: Atlantic
description: A revolutionary album that dispensed with traditional chord changes, allowing melody and collective improvisation to guide the music. Recorded in May 1959 at Radio Recorders in Hollywood with Don Cherry on cornet, Charlie Haden on bass, and Billy Higgins on drums, Coleman's pianoless quartet created a liberating new approach to jazz. 'Lonely Woman,' with its haunting melody and free-flowing rhythm, became his signature piece. The album's title prophetically announced a new direction for jazz.
significance: Launched the free jazz movement and permanently changed the boundaries of what jazz could be. Coleman's 'harmolodic' approach—treating melody, harmony, and rhythm as equals—shocked the jazz establishment but influenced countless musicians from John Coltrane to Sonic Youth. The album extends far beyond jazz, shaping rock, punk, and experimental music for decades.

title: Thelonious Alone in San Francisco
label: Riverside
description: Thelonious Alone in San Francisco is jazz pianist Thelonious Monk's third solo album, recorded in 1959.
significance: Captures pianist Thelonious Monk in a 1959 hard bop setting, adding to the recorded documentation of the hard bop era.
keyTracks: You Took the Words Right Out of My Heart

title: Time Out
label: Columbia
description: Famous for featuring unusual time signatures, Time Out introduced jazz to mainstream audiences with its accessible yet innovative approach. Recorded in 1959 with Paul Desmond on alto saxophone, Eugene Wright on bass, and Joe Morello on drums, the album explores odd meters like 9/8 ('Blue Rondo à la Turk') and 5/4 ('Take Five,' composed by Desmond). The quartet's cool, West Coast sound made complex rhythms feel natural and swinging. The distinctive album cover by Neil Fujita became an icon of modernist design.
significance: Proved that experimental concepts could be commercially successful, becoming the first jazz album to sell over one million copies. 'Take Five' remains one of the most recognized jazz compositions worldwide. The album introduced many listeners to jazz and demonstrated that rhythmic innovation could be both intellectual and accessible.

title: Blues & Roots
label: Atlantic
description: Blues & Roots is an album by Charles Mingus, recorded in 1959 and released on the Atlantic label in 1960. It has been reissued on CD by both Atlantic and Rhino.
significance: Showed that avant-garde ambitions and blues roots weren't mutually exclusive. The album's raw energy influenced countless musicians.

title: Booker Little
label: Time Records
description: Booker Little is an album by American jazz trumpeter Booker Little featuring performances recorded in 1960 for the Time label.
significance: This Time Records date features "Opening Statement" as part of Booker Little's hard bop output from 1960.

title: Cannonball Adderley and the Poll-Winners
label: Riverside
description: Cannonball Adderley and the Poll-Winners is an album by jazz saxophonist Julian "Cannonball" Adderley released on the Riverside label, featuring performances by Adderley with Wes Montgomery, Ray Brown, Victor Feldman, and Louis Hayes.
significance: A Riverside session that documents guitarist Wes Montgomery working through the cool jazz vocabulary of the cool jazz period.

title: Charles Mingus Presents Charles Mingus
label: Candid
description: Charles Mingus Presents Charles Mingus is an album by the jazz bassist and composer Charles Mingus. It was released in January 1961 through Candid Records. The recording took place in October 1960. The quartet of Mingus, multi-instrumentalist Eric Dolphy, trumpeter Ted Curson, and drummer Dannie Richmond constituted Mingus' core working band at the time, and had been performing the material on this album for weeks at The Showplace in New York.
significance: It was released in January 1961 through Candid Records.
keyTracks: All the Things You Could Be by Now If Sigmund Freud’s Wife Was Your Mother

title: Free Form
label: Jazzland
description: Jamaican-born, London-based alto saxophonist Joe Harriott independently developed free jazz at the same time as Ornette Coleman but on the other side of the Atlantic. This groundbreaking album combines Caribbean lyricism with total improvisational freedom in startling, original ways.
significance: Demonstrated that free jazz was not exclusively an American invention. Harriott's unique approach—more lyrical and less angst-ridden than his American counterparts—has been increasingly recognized as a parallel, independent achievement.

title: Giant Steps
label: Atlantic
description: Giant Steps is a studio album by the jazz musician John Coltrane. It was released in February 1960 through Atlantic Records. This was Coltrane's first album as leader for the label, with which he had signed a new contract the previous year. The record is regarded as one of the most influential jazz albums of all time. Many of its tracks have become practice templates for jazz saxophonists. In 2004, it was one of fifty recordings chosen that year by the Library of Congress to be added to the National Recording Registry. It attained gold record status in 2018, having sold 500,000 copies.
significance: The title track's chord changes remain one of the most challenging in jazz. This album marked the peak of Coltrane's 'sheets of sound' period.

title: Gillespiana
label: Verve
description: Gillespiana is an album by trumpeter Dizzy Gillespie featuring compositions and arrangements by Lalo Schifrin recorded in 1960 and released on the Verve label.
significance: Captures trumpeter Dizzy Gillespie in a 1960 bebop setting, adding to the recorded documentation of the free jazz era.

title: Jazz Epistles: Verse 1
label: Gallo
description: The Jazz Epistles were South Africa's first important bebop band. Inspired by Art Blakey's Jazz Messengers, its members included Dollar Brand on piano, Kippie Moeketsi on alto saxophone, Jonas Gwangwa on trombone, Hugh Masekela on trumpet, Johnny Gertze on bass, and Early Mabuza or Makaya Ntshoko on drums. The group became famous after performing in the jam sessions called Jazz at the Odin in the Odin Theater in Sophiatown.
significance: The only album by South Africa's first modern jazz group, recorded months before the Sharpeville massacre changed everything.

title: Louie and the Dukes of Dixieland
label: Audio Fidelity
description: I've Got the World on a String is a 1960 album by Louis Armstrong, arranged by Russell Garcia. It was recorded on the same day as Armstrong's 1958 album Louis Under the Stars; the previous day he had finished recording Ella and Louis Again with Ella Fitzgerald.
significance: It was recorded on the same day as Armstrong's 1958 album Louis Under the Stars; the previous day he had finished recording Ella and Louis Again with Ella Fitzgerald.

title: Mingus Dynasty
label: Columbia
description: Mingus Dynasty is a jazz album by Charles Mingus, recorded in 1959 and released on Columbia Records in May 1960. It is a companion album to his 1959 record Mingus Ah Um, and was inducted in the Grammy Hall of Fame in 1999. The title alludes to Mingus's ancestry, which was partially Chinese.
significance: It is a companion album to his 1959 record Mingus Ah Um, and was inducted in the Grammy Hall of Fame in 1999.
keyTracks: Things Ain’t What They Used to Be

title: Portrait in Jazz
label: Riverside
description: Portrait in Jazz is the fifth studio album by American jazz pianist Bill Evans as a leader, released in 1960. It is the first of only two studio albums to be recorded with his famous trio featuring bassist Scott LaFaro and drummer Paul Motian. It was originally released in both mono and stereo editions, each featuring a different take of "Autumn Leaves."
significance: Established the template for modern jazz piano trio playing. The three-way conversation between instruments became the gold standard.

title: Sketches of Spain
label: Columbia
description: Sketches of Spain is a studio album by American jazz musician Miles Davis, released on July 18, 1960, by Columbia Records. Recording took place between November 1959 and March 1960 at Columbia's 30th Street Studio in New York City. An extended version of the second movement of Joaquín Rodrigo's Concierto de Aranjuez (1939) is included, as well as a piece called "Will o' the Wisp", from Manuel de Falla's ballet El amor brujo (1914–1915). Sketches of Spain is regarded as an exemplary recording of third stream, a musical fusion of jazz, European classical, and styles from world music.
significance: Sketches of Spain is regarded as an exemplary recording of third stream, a musical fusion of jazz, European classical, and styles from world music.

title: Soul Junction
label: Prestige
description: A hard bop outing from 1960, Soul Junction features trumpeter Donald Byrd recording for Prestige. The set features "Soul Junction", "Woody'n You", and "Birk's Works" among its selections.
significance: As a bandleader, Byrd was an influence on the early career of Herbie Hancock and many others.

title: Soul Junction
label: Prestige
description: Lush Life is a studio album by the jazz musician John Coltrane. It was released in early 1961 by Prestige Records. It was assembled from previously unissued tracks from three recording sessions at Van Gelder Studio in Hackensack, New Jersey in 1957 and 1958. As Coltrane's profile increased during the 1960s, some years after the end of his Prestige contract, the label used unissued recordings to create new albums without Coltrane's input or approval.
significance: It was released in early 1961 by Prestige Records.

title: Soul Time
label: Riverside
description: Holiday Soul is an album of Christmas music by the American jazz pianist Bobby Timmons, recorded in 1964 and released on the Prestige label.
significance: Captures pianist Bobby Timmons during the cool jazz movement, favoring lighter timbres, relaxed tempos, and a chamber sensibility.

title: The Centaur and the Phoenix
label: Riverside
description: Detroit's Yusef Lateef was among the first jazz musicians to incorporate non-Western instruments and scales into his music. This album features his oboe, flute, and tenor saxophone alongside exotic instruments, creating a unique fusion of hard bop with Middle Eastern and Asian musical elements.
significance: A pioneering work of world jazz fusion, years before the term existed. Lateef's incorporation of Eastern instruments and modes into hard bop opened doors for the entire world music movement in jazz.

title: This Here Is Bobby Timmons
label: Riverside
description: This Here Is Bobby Timmons is an album by American jazz pianist Bobby Timmons recorded in 1960 and released on the Riverside label. It was his first album as sole leader; the earlier Jenkins, Jordan and Timmons (1957) was a co-led date with saxophonists John Jenkins and Clifford Jordan.
significance: It was his first album as sole leader; the earlier Jenkins, Jordan and Timmons (1957) was a co-led date with saxophonists John Jenkins and Clifford Jordan.

title: We Insist! Max Roach's Freedom Now Suite
label: Candid
description: We Insist! is an album by the jazz drummer and composer Max Roach which was released in January 1961 through Candid Records. It consists of a suite that Roach and lyricist Oscar Brown had begun to develop in 1959 with a view to its performance in 1963 on the centennial of the Emancipation Proclamation. The cover references the sit-in movement of the Civil Rights Movement. The Penguin Guide to Jazz awarded the album one of its rare crown accolades, in addition to featuring it as part of its Core Collection.
significance: One of the first jazz albums to directly address civil rights. Its uncompromising message influenced politically conscious jazz.

title: ... How Time Passes...
label: Candid
description: How Time Passes is the debut album by trumpeter Don Ellis recorded in 1960 and released on the Candid label.
significance: Trumpeter Don Ellis's hard bop sensibility is on display in this 1961 session, a document of the hard bop era.

title: At the Five Spot, Vol. 1
label: Prestige
description: Eric Dolphy at the Five Spot is a pair of live albums by the jazz musician and composer Eric Dolphy. They were released in December 1961 and November 1963 by Prestige Records. They were recorded on the night of 16 July 1961 at the end of Dolphy's two-week residency, alongside trumpeter Booker Little, at the Five Spot jazz club in New York. It was the only night to be recorded. The engineer was Rudy Van Gelder.
significance: Documents one of jazz's great partnerships tragically cut short. Little's mature brilliance at such a young age remains stunning.

title: Buhaina's Delight
label: Blue Note
description: Buhaina's Delight is a jazz album released by Art Blakey and the Jazz Messengers in 1963. Produced by Alfred Lion, the album was recorded in two sessions on November 28, 1961 and December 18, 1961 at Van Gelder Studio in Englewood Cliffs, New Jersey. The album was released by Blue Note Records in July 1963 after Blakey had gone to Riverside Records in late 1962.
significance: Captured the Messengers at a creative peak. Shorter's compositions showed his growing stature as a writer.

title: Eastern Sounds
label: Moodsville
description: Eastern Sounds is a studio album by the jazz multi-instrumentalist and composer Yusef Lateef. It was released in April 1962 through Prestige Records on their Moodsville imprint. The recording was made in September 1961. The album features Lateef's continued exploration of Middle Eastern music, which were incorporated into his version of hard bop with a quartet featuring Barry Harris on piano. The opening track features Lateef on Chinese globular flute, generally called xun.
significance: Pioneered world music in jazz. His multi-instrumentalism and cross-cultural approach opened new possibilities.

title: Explorations
label: Riverside
description: Explorations is an album by jazz pianist Bill Evans that was originally released by Riverside Records in 1961. It was the second and final studio album Evans recorded with his classic trio featuring Scott LaFaro on bass and Paul Motian on drums.
significance: Further developed the trio's conversational approach. The intimacy and interplay became a model for all piano trios.

title: Face to Face
label: Blue Note
description: Roosevelt 'Baby Face' Willette's debut album is a raw, gospel-drenched organ jazz session that burns with sanctified fervor. With Fred Jackson's gutbucket tenor and Grant Green's crisp guitar, the music channels the Black church directly into the nightclub.
significance: One of the rawest and most soulful organ jazz albums ever recorded. Willette's brief recording career left only a handful of albums, making this debut all the more precious.

title: Free Jazz: A Collective Improvisation
label: Atlantic
description: Free Jazz: A Collective Improvisation is a studio album by American jazz saxophonist Ornette Coleman, released in September 1961 by Atlantic Records. The recording session took place on December 21, 1960, at A&R Studios in New York City. The sole outtake from the album session, "First Take," was later released on the 1971 compilation Twins and subsequent CD reissues of Free Jazz. The album is named after the then-nascent free jazz movement.
significance: Defined the aesthetic of free jazz: collective improvisation without predetermined structure. Its influence extended to rock, punk, and experimental music.

title: Jazz Pictures at an Exibition
label: Philips
description: On Jazz Pictures at an Exibition, drummer Kenny Clarke leads a hard bop session recorded for Philips in 1961. The date includes "I'm Gonna Sit Right Down and Write Myself a Letter" and "Autumn Leaves".
significance: The date includes "I'm Gonna Sit Right Down and Write Myself a Letter" and "Autumn Leaves".
keyTracks: I'm Gonna Sit Right Down and Write Myself a Letter

title: Midnight Special
label: Blue Note
description: Midnight Special is an album by the American jazz organist Jimmy Smith, recorded in 1960 and released on the Blue Note label. The album was recorded at the same session that produced Back at the Chicken Shack (1960).
significance: The album was recorded at the same session that produced Back at the Chicken Shack (1960).

title: My Favorite Things
label: Atlantic
description: My Favorite Things is a studio album by the jazz musician John Coltrane. It was released in March 1961 on Atlantic Records. It was the first album to feature Coltrane playing soprano saxophone. An edited version of the title track became a hit single that gained popularity in 1961 on radio. The record became a major commercial success.
significance: It was the first album to feature Coltrane playing soprano saxophone.

title: Out Front
label: Candid
description: Out Front is a 1961 album by American jazz trumpeter Booker Little featuring performances recorded and released by the Candid label.
significance: Features "We Speak" and "Strength and Sanity" in a hard bop context, highlighting Booker Little's interpretive range.

title: Percussion Bitter Sweet
label: Impulse!
description: Percussion Bitter Sweet is an album by jazz drummer Max Roach recorded in 1961, released on Impulse! Records. It was trumpeter Booker Little's penultimate recording before he died from uremia in early October 1961.
significance: It was trumpeter Booker Little's penultimate recording before he died from uremia in early October 1961.

title: Rights of Swing
label: Candid
description: Rights of Swing is an album led by saxophonist Phil Woods featuring performances recorded in early 1961 and originally released on the Candid label.
significance: Captures clarinetist Phil Woods during the hard bop period, reconnecting jazz with blues and gospel while keeping bebop's sophistication.

title: Screamin' the Blues
label: Prestige
description: Screamin' the Blues is an album by American saxophonist Oliver Nelson, originally released in 1961 on New Jazz Records.
significance: Part of Prestige's hard bop catalog. Prestige under Bob Weinstock was known for its prolific output of blowing sessions, often recording a full album in a single day.

title: Shirley Scott Plays Horace Silver
label: Prestige
description: Shirley Scott Plays Horace Silver is an album by organist Shirley Scott featuring compositions by Horace Silver which was recorded in 1961 and released on the Prestige label.
significance: Demonstrated that the jazz organ could be soulful and sophisticated. Scott's influence on soul jazz organists was immense.

title: Shirley's Sounds
label: Prestige
description: Shirley's Sounds is a studio album by organist Shirley Scott recorded mainly in 1958 for Prestige but not released until 1961 as PRLP 7195.
significance: Released by Prestige during the hard bop period, reconnecting jazz with blues and gospel while keeping bebop's sophistication, this set captures Shirley Scott in characteristic form.
keyTracks: There Will Never Be Another You

title: Straight Ahead
label: Candid
description: Straight Ahead is an album by American jazz vocalist Abbey Lincoln featuring performances recorded in 1961 for the Candid label.
significance: Part of the cultural moment that included We Insist! The music insists that jazz vocals can carry political weight and formal innovation.

title: Sunday at the Village Vanguard
label: Riverside
description: Sunday at the Village Vanguard is a live album by jazz pianist and composer Bill Evans and his Trio consisting of Evans, bassist Scott LaFaro, and drummer Paul Motian. Released in 1961, the album is routinely ranked as one of the best live jazz recordings of all time.
significance: Redefined the piano trio format. The three-way conversation between piano, bass, and drums became the model for all jazz piano trios that followed.

title: The Honeydripper
label: Prestige
description: Brother Jack McDuff's signature album features a young Grant Green on guitar in a greasy, funky organ combo setting. The title track's swaggering groove is irresistible, and McDuff's percussive Hammond B-3 attack set him apart from the smoother Jimmy Smith.
significance: Established McDuff as a major force in organ jazz. The album's hard-swinging, blues-drenched approach influenced the entire soul jazz movement and anticipated jazz-funk.

title: Thelonious Monk with John Coltrane
label: Jazzland
description: Thelonious Monk with John Coltrane is a 1961 album by Thelonious Monk issued on Jazzland Records, a subsidiary of Riverside Records. It consists of material recorded four years earlier when Monk worked extensively with John Coltrane, issued after Coltrane had become a leader and jazz star in his own right.
significance: It consists of material recorded four years earlier when Monk worked extensively with John Coltrane, issued after Coltrane had become a leader and jazz star in his own right.

title: Together!
label: Atlantic
description: Together! is a jazz album by drummers ”Philly” Joe Jones and Elvin Jones recorded in 1961 and released on the Atlantic label. It features trumpeter Blue Mitchell, trombonist Curtis Fuller, tenor saxophonist Hank Mobley, pianist Wynton Kelly and bassist Paul Chambers.
significance: His brothers Hank and Thad were also celebrated jazz musicians with whom he occasionally recorded.

title: Waltz for Debby
label: Riverside
description:
Waltz for Debby is the name of:"Waltz for Debby" (song), a 1956 jazz composition by Bill Evans later made into a song
Waltz for Debby , album by Bill Evans
Waltz for Debby , album by Monica Zetterlund and Bill Evans
significance: Demonstrated the piano trio's potential for intimate, chamber-like jazz. Its influence on modern jazz piano is incalculable.

title: Where?
label: New Jazz
description: Where? is the debut album by bassist Ron Carter recorded in 1961 at Van Gelder Studio and released on the New Jazz label. Some reissues of the album appear under Eric Dolphy's name.
significance: is the debut album by bassist Ron Carter recorded in 1961 at Van Gelder Studio and released on the New Jazz label.
keyTracks: Softly, as in a Morning Sunrise

title: Carmen McRae Sings Lover Man
label: Columbia
description: Carmen McRae Sings Lover Man and Other Billie Holiday Classics is a 1962 studio album by Carmen McRae, recorded in tribute to McRae's idol, Billie Holiday, who had died two years previously.
significance: One of the finest Holiday tribute albums. McRae's diction and timing were unmatched among jazz singers of her generation.

title: Dear Lonely Hearts
label: Capitol Records
description: Dear Lonely Hearts is a 1962 studio album by Nat King Cole, arranged by Belford Hendricks. The LP peaked at number 24 on the Billboard albums chart. The title track reached number two for two weeks on the Easy Listening charts and number 13 on the Hot 100 singles chart.
significance: The LP peaked at number 24 on the Billboard albums chart.
keyTracks: Why Should I Cry Over You?

title: Far Cry
label: New Jazz
description: Far Cry is the third album by jazz musician Eric Dolphy, released in 1962 on New Jazz Featuring a quintet co-led with trumpeter Booker Little, it is one of the few recordings of their partnership. Pianist Jaki Byard, bassist Ron Carter and drummer Roy Haynes all return from earlier Dolphy albums. This was a busy time for Dolphy- he took part in Ornette Coleman's Free Jazz session and recorded this album on the same day, and had participated in the Jazz Abstractions project the previous day.
significance: Features some of Dolphy's most lyrical playing alongside his more angular explorations. The album balances accessibility with adventure.

title: Go!
label: Blue Note
description: Go! is a studio album by jazz musician Dexter Gordon featuring Sonny Clark, Butch Warren and Billy Higgins, recorded on August 27, 1962 and released in the same year on Blue Note. According to the liner notes by Ira Gitler, this session was "not recorded in a nightclub performance but, in its informal symmetry, it matches the relaxed atmosphere that the best of those made in that manner engender. Everyone was really together, in all the most positive meanings of that word." It was recorded by Rudy Van Gelder at the Van Gelder Studio in Englewood Cliffs.
significance: Marked Gordon's comeback and reminded the jazz world of his importance. His version of 'Cheese Cake' became definitive.
keyTracks: I Guess I'll Hang My Tears Out to Dry

title: Hub-Tones
label: Blue Note
description: Hub-Tones is an album by trumpeter Freddie Hubbard recorded on October 10, 1962, and released on the Blue Note label as BLP 4115 and BST 84115. It contains performances by Hubbard, James Spaulding, Herbie Hancock, Reggie Workman and Clifford Jarvis. The cover artwork was designed by Reid Miles with photography by Francis Wolff.
significance: Established Hubbard as hard bop's premier trumpeter after Clifford Brown. His technique and ideas were groundbreaking.

title: Let Freedom Ring
label: Blue Note
description: McLean's breakthrough album found the alto saxophonist pushing beyond conventional hard bop into freer territory, inspired by Ornette Coleman. With Walter Davis Jr., Herbie Lewis, and Billy Higgins, McLean's sharp, cry-like tone navigates between bebop tradition and the new thing.
significance: A crucial transitional album that showed how mainstream jazz musicians could absorb the lessons of free jazz without abandoning structure. McLean's emotional directness made the avant-garde accessible.

title: Money Jungle
label: United Artists Jazz
description: Money Jungle is a studio album by the pianist Duke Ellington with the double bassist Charles Mingus and the drummer Max Roach. It was recorded on September 17, 1962, and released in February 1963 by United Artists Jazz. All but one of the compositions were written by Ellington, with four of the seven on the original LP being recorded for the first time on this album. Later releases on CD added eight tracks from the same recording session.
significance: All but one of the compositions were written by Ellington, with four of the seven on the original LP being recorded for the first time on this album.

title: Nancy Wilson / Cannonball Adderley
label: Capitol Records
description: Somethin' Else is an album by American jazz saxophonist Julian "Cannonball" Adderley, recorded on March 9, 1958 and released on Blue Note in August later that year—his only album for the label. Also on the session is trumpeter Miles Davis in one of his handful of recording dates for Blue Note. Adderley was a member of Davis' group at the time, and the album was recorded shortly after Davis' own landmark album Milestones.
significance: Adderley was a member of Davis' group at the time, and the album was recorded shortly after Davis' own landmark album Milestones.

title: Ready for Freddie
label: Blue Note
description: Ready for Freddie is an album by trumpeter Freddie Hubbard, released on the Blue Note label in 1962 as BLP 4085 and BST 84085. In 2003, it was remastered and issued on CD with the addition of two alternate takes. It contains performances by Hubbard, Bernard McKinney, Wayne Shorter, McCoy Tyner, Art Davis and Elvin Jones.
significance: In 2003, it was remastered and issued on CD with the addition of two alternate takes.

title: Stop and Listen
label: Blue Note
description: Stop and Listen is the second album by American jazz organist Baby Face Willette featuring performances recorded and released on the Blue Note label in 1961.
