It features a version of the 1941 Harry Warren song "At Last," which at the time of the Stop and Listen recording sessions was on the R&B and Billboard Hot 100 record charts through Etta James' hit recording of the song.
significance: It features a version of the 1941 Harry Warren song "At Last," which at the time of the Stop and Listen recording sessions was on the R&B and Billboard Hot 100 record charts through Etta James' hit recording of the song.

title: Takin' Off
label: Blue Note
description: Takin' Off is the debut album by jazz pianist Herbie Hancock released in 1962 by Blue Note Records. The album features veteran tenor saxophonist Dexter Gordon, trumpeter Freddie Hubbard, bassist Butch Warren and drummer Billy Higgins. The album is a creative example of music in the hard bop idiom. The bluesy track "Watermelon Man" made it to the Top 100 of the singles charts, and went on to become a jazz standard. Hancock released a funk arrangement of “Watermelon Man” on his 1973 album Head Hunters.
significance: Launched Hancock's extraordinary career. 'Watermelon Man' proved jazz could be funky and accessible.

title: The Bridge
label: RCA Victor
description: The Bridge is a studio album by jazz saxophonist Sonny Rollins, recorded in 1962. It was Rollins's first release following a three-year sabbatical and was his first album for RCA Victor. The saxophonist was joined by the musicians with whom he recorded for the next segment of his career: Jim Hall on guitar, Bob Cranshaw on bass, and Ben Riley on drums.
significance: Documented one of jazz's great comebacks. Rollins emerged with renewed vigor and a broader musical palette.

title: The Newest Sound Around
label: RCA Victor
description: Pianist Ran Blake and vocalist Jeanne Lee's radical debut reimagines pop standards and gospel songs as stark, intimate noir miniatures. Lee's ethereal, speech-like singing and Blake's dark, Monk-influenced piano create an atmosphere of haunting beauty and psychological tension.
significance: One of the most original vocal-piano duo recordings in jazz history. The album's 'third stream' approach—blending jazz, classical, and folk—was decades ahead of its time and has been increasingly recognized as a masterpiece.

title: Think Well of Me
label: Unknown
description: Think Well of Me is a 1962 studio album by trombonist/vocalist Jack Teagarden, performing songs by Willard Robison, accompanied by an orchestra arranged and conducted by Bob Brookmeyer or Russ Case, that was released by the Verve label.
significance: A contemporary jazz entry from 1962 by Jack Teagarden, documenting the breadth of the swing period.

title: What’s New?
label: RCA Victor
description: The Bridge is a studio album by jazz saxophonist Sonny Rollins, recorded in 1962. It was Rollins's first release following a three-year sabbatical and was his first album for RCA Victor. The saxophonist was joined by the musicians with whom he recorded for the next segment of his career: Jim Hall on guitar, Bob Cranshaw on bass, and Ben Riley on drums.
significance: It was Rollins's first release following a three-year sabbatical and was his first album for RCA Victor.
keyTracks: If Ever I Would Leave You
The Night Has a Thousand Eyes

title: Back at the Chicken Shack
label: Blue Note
description: Back at the Chicken Shack is an album by Jimmy Smith. It was recorded in 1960 and released in 1963 on the Blue Note label. Smith recorded the album in the same session as his previous album Midnight Special. It was cited in the book 1001 Albums You Must Hear Before You Die.
significance: The quintessential organ jazz album. Smith's greasy, soulful playing defined the sound of a genre and influenced every organist who followed, from Brother Jack McDuff to Dr. Lonnie Smith.
keyTracks: When I Grow Too Old to Dream

title: Blue Bash!
label: Verve
description: Blue Bash! is an album by the guitarist Kenny Burrell and organist Jimmy Smith, recorded in 1963 and released on the Verve label.
significance: is an album by the guitarist Kenny Burrell and organist Jimmy Smith, recorded in 1963 and released on the Verve label.

title: Blue Bash!
label: Verve
description: Blue Bash! is an album by the guitarist Kenny Burrell and organist Jimmy Smith, recorded in 1963 and released on the Verve label.
significance: is an album by the guitarist Kenny Burrell and organist Jimmy Smith, recorded in 1963 and released on the Verve label.

title: Boss Guitar
label: Riverside
description: Boss Guitar is an album by American jazz guitarist Wes Montgomery, released in 1963 by Riverside. It was reissued on the Original Jazz Classics label with alternate takes. All the tracks are available on Wes Montgomery: The Complete Riverside Recordings.
significance: It was reissued on the Original Jazz Classics label with alternate takes.

title: Caravan
label: Riverside
description: Art Blakey and the Jazz Messengers is a studio album by Art Blakey and the Jazz Messengers, released on September 1, 1961, through Impulse! Records. Expanding to a sextet for the first time, it was the group's final recording with Bobby Timmons, who would be replaced by Cedar Walton.
significance: Showed the Messengers' versatility and ability to transform standards into hard bop vehicles.

title: Conversations
label: FM
description: Conversations is a 1963 album by American jazz multi-instrumentalist, Eric Dolphy first released by the FM label and later reissued by Vee-Jay as The Eric Dolphy Memorial Album the following year. The album was reissued on disc one of Musical Prophet: The Expanded 1963 New York Studio Sessions, released in 2018 by Resonance Records.
significance: The album was reissued on disc one of Musical Prophet: The Expanded 1963 New York Studio Sessions, released in 2018 by Resonance Records.

title: Duke Ellington Meets Coleman Hawkins
label: impulse!
description: Duke Ellington Meets Coleman Hawkins is a jazz album by Duke Ellington and Coleman Hawkins that was recorded on August 18, 1962, and released in February 1963 by Impulse! Records.
significance: A 1963 big band date for impulse! that documents Duke Ellington's ongoing work in the idiom.

title: Duke Ellington Meets Coleman Hawkins
label: impulse!
description: Duke Ellington Meets Coleman Hawkins is a jazz album by Duke Ellington and Coleman Hawkins that was recorded on August 18, 1962, and released in February 1963 by Impulse! Records.
significance: A big band recording from the hard bop period, reconnecting jazz with blues and gospel while keeping bebop's sophistication. Coleman Hawkins adds a personal voice to the idiom.

title: Midnight Blue
label: Blue Note
description: Kenny Burrell's most celebrated album is a late-night masterpiece of blues-drenched jazz guitar. With Ray Barretto's subtle conga accents and Stanley Turrentine's smoky tenor on half the tracks, the album creates an atmosphere of intimate, after-hours cool that is utterly seductive.
significance: The definitive jazz guitar mood album and one of Blue Note's most enduring classics. Burrell's elegant, bluesy style influenced every subsequent generation of jazz guitarists, and 'Chitlins con Carne' became a jazz standard.

title: Money Jungle
label: United Artists
description: Money Jungle is a studio album by the pianist Duke Ellington with the double bassist Charles Mingus and the drummer Max Roach. It was recorded on September 17, 1962, and released in February 1963 by United Artists Jazz. All but one of the compositions were written by Ellington, with four of the seven on the original LP being recorded for the first time on this album. Later releases on CD added eight tracks from the same recording session.
significance: Proved that Ellington could hold his own with the avant-garde. The generational clash produced some of the most exciting music of all three careers.

title: Monk's Dream
label: Columbia
description: Monk's Dream is an album by jazz pianist Thelonious Monk, released by Columbia Records in January 1963. It was Monk's first album for Columbia following his five-year recording period with Riverside Records.
significance: Marked Monk's commercial breakthrough without compromising his unique vision. His singular style remained intact.

title: No Room for Squares
label: Blue Note
description: Mobley's finest hour as a leader, featuring Lee Morgan on trumpet and either Andrew Hill or Herbie Hancock on piano. The title track's hip, angular theme perfectly captures Mobley's understated cool, while his improvisations flow with an effortless logic that rewards close listening.
significance: The definitive document of Mobley's 'middleweight' tenor style—less aggressive than Rollins, less ethereal than Coltrane, but possessed of a singing tone and melodic inventiveness that made him Blue Note's most reliable saxophonist.

title: One Step Beyond
label: Blue Note
description: One Step Beyond is an album by American saxophonist Jackie McLean, recorded in 1963 and released on the Blue Note label. This is the first of two albums where McLean replaced the usual pianist with vibraphone player Bobby Hutcherson. As a result, One Step Beyond features a new sound that, while still rooted in hard bop, was more spacious and adventurous than his earlier work and leaned towards post bop and free jazz. The group is rounded out by trombonist Grachan Moncur III, bassist Eddie Khan and drummer Tony Williams.
significance: Introduced Grachan Moncur III to the jazz world. Williams, just 17, demonstrated the revolutionary drumming that would transform Miles's second quintet.

title: Our Man in Paris
label: Blue Note
description: Our Man in Paris is a 1963 jazz album by saxophonist Dexter Gordon. The album's title refers to where the recording was made, Gordon teaming up with fellow expatriates Bud Powell and Kenny Clarke, both Parisian residents, and native Parisian Pierre Michelot. Powell, Clarke and Michelot, under the name The Three Bosses, had played together often in Paris since Powell moved there in 1959.
significance: Documented Gordon's European renaissance. His relaxed, storytelling approach influenced countless tenor players including Joe Henderson and Michael Brecker.

title: Sassy Swings the Tivoli
label: Mercury
description: Sassy Swings the Tivoli is a 1963 live album by American jazz singer Sarah Vaughan and her trio, produced by Quincy Jones. The performances were recorded in the concert hall of the Tivoli Gardens, Copenhagen, over four days in July 1963.
significance: The finest live document of Vaughan's genius. Her scatting and rhythmic daring rival any instrumentalist.
keyTracks: What Is This Thing Called Love?

title: Stan Getz With Guest Artist Laurindo Almeida
label: Verve
description: Stan Getz With Guest Artist Laurindo Almeida is an album by American saxophonist Stan Getz and guitarist Laurindo Almeida, recorded in 1963 and issued on Verve as V/V6-8665.
significance: A latin jazz entry from 1963 by Stan Getz, documenting the breadth of the cool jazz period.

title: The Black Saint and the Sinner Lady
label: Impulse!
description: The Black Saint and the Sinner Lady is a studio album by Charles Mingus. It was recorded on January 20, 1963, and released in July of that year by Impulse! Records. The album comprises a single continuous composition—partially written as a ballet—divided into four tracks and six movements. It is widely regarded as one of the greatest jazz records of all time.
significance: Pushed the boundaries of jazz composition into orchestral territory. The album's liner notes, written by Mingus's psychiatrist, added to its mystique.

title: The Latin Bit
label: Blue Note
description: The Latin Bit is an album by American jazz guitarist Grant Green featuring performances recorded in 1962 and released on the Blue Note label. It is a loose concept album inspired by Latin American music. It features tenor saxophonist Ike Quebec, pianists Sonny Clark and John Adriano Acea, bassist Wendell Marshall and percussionists Willie Bobo, Garvin Masseaux and “Patato” Valdes.
significance: It is a loose concept album inspired by Latin American music.

title: Black Christ of the Andes
label: Saba
description: Mary Lou Williams, alternatively titled Black Christ of the Andes, is a jazz album by pianist Mary Lou Williams, which was released in 1964 by Folkways Records. Released after Williams's conversion to Catholicism, the album incorporates a variety of styles from spirituals, blues and avant-garde jazz. The title track is a choral Mass hymn in honor of the Afro-Peruvian saint St. Martin de Porres.
significance: Pioneered the jazz mass genre later explored by Duke Ellington and Dave Brubeck. Williams's spiritual commitment gave the music authenticity and depth.

title: Black Fire
label: Blue Note
description: Black Fire is a studio album by American jazz pianist and composer Andrew Hill, released on Blue Note Records in 1964. It was Hill's debut for the label. Initially, drummer Philly Joe Jones was scheduled to play on the album, but was replaced by Roy Haynes after scheduling issues. The rest of the band consists of tenor saxophonist Joe Henderson and bassist Richard Davis. The Allmusic review by Stephen Thomas Erlewine calls the album "an impressive statement of purpose that retains much of its power decades after its initial release... a modern jazz classic."
significance: Announced a major new voice in jazz composition. Hill's approach—neither fully free nor strictly bop—created a third path that influenced countless musicians seeking their own voice.

title: Blue Flames
label: Prestige
description: Blue Flames is a collaboration album by organist Shirley Scott and saxophonist Stanley Turrentine recorded in 1964 and released on the Prestige label.
significance: Documented one of jazz's great partnerships. The Scott-Turrentine sound defined soul jazz organ combos.

title: Blue Notes in Concert
label: Gallo
description: Harold Melvin & the Blue Notes were an American soul and R&B vocal group. One of the most popular Philadelphia soul groups of the 1970s, the group's repertoire included soul, R&B, doo-wop, and disco. Founded in Philadelphia, Pennsylvania, in the middle of the 1950s as The Charlemagnes, the group is most noted for several hits on Gamble and Huff's Philadelphia International label between 1972 and 1976, although they performed and recorded until Melvin's death in 1997. Despite group founder and original lead singer Harold Melvin's top billing, the Blue Notes' most famous member was Teddy Pendergrass, their lead singer during the successful years at Philadelphia International.
significance: Captured South African jazz at its peak before apartheid forced musicians into exile. The Blue Notes became the nucleus of the London jazz scene.

title: Crescent
label: impulse!
description: Crescent is a studio album by the jazz musician and composer John Coltrane. It was released in July 1964 through the label Impulse!. Alongside Coltrane on tenor saxophone, the album features McCoy Tyner (piano), Jimmy Garrison and Elvin Jones (drums) playing original Coltrane compositions.
significance: It was released in July 1964 through the label Impulse!.

title: Destination...Out!
label: Blue Note
description: Destination... Out! is an album by American saxophonist Jackie McLean, recorded in 1963 and released on the Blue Note label. It is the second McLean album to feature Bobby Hutcherson on vibraphone and Grachan Moncur III on trombone. The rhythm section is completed by bassist Larry Ridley and drummer Roy Haynes.
significance: Documented McLean's brave transition from hard bop to the avant-garde. His cry-like alto became more emotional and less predictable, inspiring future generations of saxophonists.

title: Empyrean Isles
label: Blue Note
description: Empyrean Isles is the fourth studio album by American jazz pianist Herbie Hancock, released on Blue Note Records in November 1964. The album features Hancock alongside trumpeter Freddie Hubbard, bassist Ron Carter, and drummer Tony Williams.
significance: Bridged hard bop and the emerging jazz-funk sound. 'Cantaloupe Island' became a hip-hop staple decades later.

title: Evolution
label: Blue Note
description: Evolution is the debut album led by the American trombonist Grachan Moncur III, recorded in 1963 and released on the Blue Note label. Featuring alto saxophonist Jackie McLean, trumpeter Lee Morgan, vibraphonist Bobby Hutcherson, bassist Bob Cranshaw and drummer Tony Williams, Evolution is considered a significant contribution to the jazz avant-garde. Two McLean albums also recorded for Blue Note in 1963 featured Moncur and his compositions, and explored the same "inside/outside" musical approach.
significance: A overlooked masterpiece of 1960s jazz. Moncur created a new vocabulary for the trombone that influenced Roswell Rudd and later avant-garde trombonists.

title: Getz/Gilberto
label: Verve
description: Getz/Gilberto is an album by American saxophonist Stan Getz and Brazilian guitarist João Gilberto, featuring pianist and composer Antônio Carlos Jobim, who also composed many of the tracks. It was released in March 1964 by Verve Records. The album features the vocals of Astrud Gilberto on two tracks, "Garota de Ipanema" and "Corcovado". The artwork was done by artist Olga Albizu. Getz/Gilberto is a jazz and bossa nova album and includes tracks such as "Desafinado", "Corcovado", and "Garota de Ipanema". The last received a Grammy Award for Record of the Year and started Astrud Gilberto's career.
significance: Created a new genre by fusing Brazilian rhythms with American jazz. Its commercial success opened doors for world music in jazz.

title: Hello, Dolly!
label: Kapp Records
description: Hello Dolly! is a jazz album by Louis Armstrong and His All Stars, which at the time included Joe Darensbourg, Billy Kyle, Big Chief Russell Moore, Arvell Shaw, Danny Barcelona and Trummy Young. The tracks were mostly recorded on December 3, 1963, and April 18, 1964, in New York City. It was released by Kapp Records in 1964 and became Armstrong's most commercially successful album, it topped the Billboard Top LPs chart, during a seventy four-week stay on the chart.
significance: It was released by Kapp Records in 1964 and became Armstrong's most commercially successful album, it topped the Billboard Top LPs chart, during a seventy four-week stay on the chart.
keyTracks: It's Been a Long, Long Time
A Lot of Livin' to Do
A Kiss to Build a Dream On

title: Judgment!
label: Blue Note
description: Judgment! is a studio album by American jazz pianist Andrew Hill, recorded and released in 1964 on Blue Note Records. Accompanied by drummer Elvin Jones, bassist Richard Davis and vibraphonist Bobby Hutcherson, Hill weaves his music around complex harmonic structures.
significance: Solidified Hill's reputation as Blue Note's most daring composer. The interaction between Hutcherson's vibes and Hill's piano created a new sonic template.

title: Mingus Mingus Mingus Mingus Mingus
label: impulse!
description: Mingus Mingus Mingus Mingus Mingus is a studio album by the American jazz composer and bassist Charles Mingus which was released on January 9, 1964.
significance: From the hard bop period, reconnecting jazz with blues and gospel while keeping bebop's sophistication, this album positions Charles Mingus within the hard bop conversation of the time.
keyTracks: Better Get Hit in Yo’ Soul

title: Night Dreamer
label: Blue Note
description: Night Dreamer is the fourth album by American jazz saxophonist Wayne Shorter. It was released in November 1964 by Blue Note Records. With a quintet of trumpeter Lee Morgan, pianist McCoy Tyner, bassist Reggie Workman and drummer Elvin Jones performing six Shorter originals.
significance: Marked the beginning of Shorter's extraordinary run of Blue Note albums. His compositions became jazz standards.

title: Out to Lunch!
label: Blue Note
description: Out to Lunch! is a 1964 album by jazz multi-instrumentalist Eric Dolphy. His only recording on Blue Note as a leader, it was issued as BLP 4163 and BST 84163. Featuring Dolphy in a quintet with trumpeter Freddie Hubbard, vibraphonist Bobby Hutcherson, bassist Richard Davis, and drummer Tony Williams, it is considered by critics as one of the finest albums issued on Blue Note and one of the high points of 1960s avant-garde jazz. The album cover designed by Reid Miles features a photo of a "Will Be Back" sign displayed in a shop window showing a seven-handed clock.
significance: A landmark of avant-garde jazz that remained accessible. Dolphy's multi-instrumentalism and compositional vision were ahead of their time.

title: Poetry and Jazz in Concert (Record One)
label: Argo
description: Joseph Arthurlin Harriott was a Jamaican jazz musician and composer, whose principal instrument was the alto saxophone. According to George McKay in Circular Breathing: The Cultural Politics of Jazz in Britain, Harriott was 'responsible for a series of brilliant experiments in new music in Britain through the 1960s'. His work was 'crucial' in two areas of innovation: free music and then global music fusion.
significance: According to George McKay in Circular Breathing: The Cultural Politics of Jazz in Britain, Harriott was 'responsible for a series of brilliant experiments in new music in Britain through the 1960s'.

title: Prayer Meetin’
label: Blue Note
description: Prayer Meetin' is an album by the American jazz organist Jimmy Smith, recorded in 1963 and released on the Blue Note label. The album was rereleased on CD with two bonus tracks from the same session.
significance: The album was rereleased on CD with two bonus tracks from the same session.
keyTracks: Stone Cold Dead in the Market
When the Saints Go Marching In

title: Search for the New Land
label: Blue Note
description: Search for the New Land is an album by jazz trumpeter Lee Morgan. A set with a group of regular Blue Note sidemen, Search for the New Land was recorded before The Sidewinder was released. Although it was recorded in 1964, the album was shelved for two years, then issued with the original catalogue number 84169.
significance: Showed Morgan's growth as a composer and thinker. The title track's mysterious mood was innovative for hard bop.

title: The Most Important Jazz Album of 1964/65
label: Colpix
description: The Most Important Jazz Album of 1964/65 is an album by trumpeter/vocalist Chet Baker which was recorded in 1964 and released on the Colpix label.
significance: A cool jazz entry from 1964 by Chet Baker, documenting the breadth of the free jazz period.

title: The Sidewinder
label: Blue Note
description: The Sidewinder is a 1964 album by the jazz trumpeter Lee Morgan, recorded at the Van Gelder Studio in Englewood Cliffs, New Jersey, U.S. It was released on the Blue Note label as BLP 4157 (mono) and BST 84157 (stereo).
significance: Demonstrated that artistic jazz could also be commercially viable. The 'Sidewinder' beat influenced countless soul jazz recordings.

title: Witches & Devils
label: Debut
description: Spirits is an album by American free jazz saxophonist Albert Ayler recorded in New York City in 1964 and first released on the Danish Debut label then later released on the Freedom label as Witches & Devils.
significance: Released by Debut during the free jazz era, when musicians abandoned predetermined structures for collective expression, this set captures Albert Ayler in characteristic form.

title: A Caddy for Daddy
label: Blue Note
description: A superb session featuring Lee Morgan and Curtis Fuller alongside Mobley's burnished tenor. The title track's soulful groove and 'The Morning After' are quintessential mid-60s Blue Note, balancing sophistication with deep swing.
significance: Showcases Mobley at his most compositionally mature, writing tunes that are both catchy and harmonically adventurous. The album captures the last golden moment of classic Blue Note hard bop.

title: A Love Supreme
label: Impulse!
description: A four-part suite that represents Coltrane's spiritual awakening and devotion to God. Recorded in a single session on December 9, 1964, at Van Gelder Studio in New Jersey with his classic quartet—McCoy Tyner on piano, Jimmy Garrison on bass, and Elvin Jones on drums—the album blends complex improvisation with profound emotional and spiritual depth. The famous four-note motif ('A Love Supreme') that opens 'Acknowledgement' becomes a meditative chant, while 'Psalm' features Coltrane 'playing' the words of a poem he wrote for the liner notes.
significance: One of the most influential albums in jazz history, demonstrating that jazz could be a vehicle for deep spiritual expression. The album bridged modal jazz and the emerging free jazz movement, selling over one million copies and inspiring musicians from Carlos Santana to Kendrick Lamar. It earned Coltrane a devoted cult following that persists today.

reviews.quote: A Love Supreme is one of the landmark recordings in jazz history, a series of incantations and prayers that sounds like a man struggling to find God.
Coltrane's most famous album is a deeply spiritual work that transcends genre boundaries.

title: Behind the 8 Ball
label: Argo
description: Face to Face is the debut album by jazz organist Baby Face Willette featuring performances recorded and released on the Blue Note label in 1961.
significance: From the hard bop period, reconnecting jazz with blues and gospel while keeping bebop's sophistication, this album positions Baby Face Willette within the soul jazz conversation of the time.

title: Blue Note 4000
label: Blue Note
description: Freddie Hubbard, trumpeter, recorded Blue Note 4000 in 1965 for Blue Note. The set features "Open Sesame", "Hub-Tones", and "Ready for Freddie" among its selections. The album comes from the hard bop period, reconnecting jazz with blues and gospel while keeping bebop's sophistication.
significance: Hubbard brought new energy to hard bop trumpet, bridging the gap between Clifford Brown and the avant-garde.

title: Dippin'
label: Blue Note
description: One of Mobley's most soulful outings, built around the irresistible groove of the title track, which became a Blue Note classic. The quintet with Lee Morgan and Harold Mabern Jr. swings with infectious joy throughout.
significance: Contains Mobley's most recognizable composition in the title track, a boogaloo-tinged burner that became a staple of jazz DJs and rare groove collectors worldwide.

title: Duke Ellington, Fletcher Henderson, Artie Shaw and Their Orchestras
label: Society
description: A big band outing from 1965, Duke Ellington, Fletcher Henderson, Artie Shaw and Their Orchestras features pianist Fletcher Henderson recording for Society. Among the tracks are "Molten Swing" and "Minor Riff".
significance: Among the tracks are "Molten Swing" and "Minor Riff".

title: E.S.P.
label: Columbia
description: E.S.P. is an album by Miles Davis, recorded on January 20–22, 1965 and released on August 16 of that year by Columbia Records. It is the first release from what is known as Davis's second great quintet: Davis on trumpet, Wayne Shorter on tenor saxophone, Herbie Hancock on piano, Ron Carter on bass, and Tony Williams on drums. The album was named after a tune by Shorter, and was inspired by the fact that, "since Wayne Shorter's arrival, the five members of the quintet seemed to communicate by mental telepathy."
significance: It is the first release from what is known as Davis's second great quintet: Davis on trumpet, Wayne Shorter on tenor saxophone, Herbie Hancock on piano, Ron Carter on bass, and Tony Williams on drums.

title: Fire Music
label: Impulse!
description: Fire Music is a studio album by jazz saxophonist Archie Shepp released on Impulse! Records in 1965. "Malcolm, Malcolm Semper Malcolm" is dedicated to Malcolm X, whilst "Los Olvidados" is a homage to the 1950 film of the same name. Featured musicians include trumpeter Ted Curson, trombonist Joe Orange, alto saxophonist Marion Brown, bassist Reggie Johnson and drummer Joe Chambers.
significance: Merged Black nationalist politics with avant-garde music more directly than any previous jazz record. Shepp became the most politically outspoken of the New Thing musicians.

title: Fuchsia Swing Song
label: Blue Note
description: Fuchsia Swing Song is the debut album by American saxophonist Sam Rivers recorded in 1964 and released on the Blue Note label. The album was reissued on CD in 1995, and again in 2003 as part of the "Connoisseur Series" including four alternate takes as bonus tracks.
significance: Introduced Rivers as a major voice in the new music. His approach—rooted in tradition but reaching toward freedom—influenced the loft jazz movement he would later help create.

title: I Put a Spell on You
label: Philips
description: I Put a Spell on You is a studio album by American singer, songwriter, arranger and pianist Nina Simone. Recorded in 1964 and 1965 in New York City, it was released by Philips Records in 1965. It peaked at number 99 on the Billboard 200 chart and number 18 on the UK Albums Chart. The title track "I Put a Spell on You" peaked at number 23 on the Hot Rhythm & Blues Singles chart and number 28 on the UK Singles Chart.
significance: It peaked at number 99 on the Billboard 200 chart and number 18 on the UK Albums Chart.
keyTracks: I Put a Spell on You

title: Idle Moments
label: Blue Note
description: Idle Moments is an album by American jazz guitarist Grant Green recorded in 1963 and released on the Blue Note label in 1965. It features performances by tenor saxophonist Joe Henderson, vibraphonist Bobby Hutcherson, pianist Duke Pearson, bassist Bob Cranshaw and drummer Al Harewood.
significance: Showed that jazz could be spacious and meditative without losing its soul. Green's understated guitar was hypnotic.

title: Inner Urge
label: Blue Note
description: Inner Urge is an album by the jazz saxophonist Joe Henderson, released in 1966 via Blue Note Records, his fourth recorded as a leader. It was recorded at the Van Gelder Studio, Englewood Cliffs, New Jersey, on November 30, 1964. Featuring Henderson along with pianist McCoy Tyner and drummer Elvin Jones, and bassist Bob Cranshaw.
significance: Showed that hard bop could be adventurous without abandoning its roots. Henderson's influence on later saxophonists was immense.

title: JuJu
label: Blue Note
description: JuJu is the fifth album by American jazz saxophonist Wayne Shorter. It was released in July 1965 by Blue Note Records. It features a rhythm section of pianist McCoy Tyner, bassist Reggie Workman and drummer Elvin Jones, all of whom had worked extensively with Shorter's fellow tenor saxophonist John Coltrane.
significance: Established Shorter's signature style: moody, oblique, and unforgettable. The album influenced the direction of jazz composition.

title: Maiden Voyage
label: Blue Note
description: Maiden Voyage is the fifth album led by jazz musician Herbie Hancock, and was recorded by Rudy Van Gelder on March 17, 1965, for Blue Note Records. It was issued as BLP 4195 and BST 84195. Featuring Hancock with tenor saxophonist George Coleman, trumpeter Freddie Hubbard, bassist Ron Carter and drummer Tony Williams, it is a concept album aimed at creating an oceanic atmosphere. As such, many of the track titles refer to marine biology or the sea, and the musicians develop the concept through their use of space. The album was presented with the Grammy Hall of Fame Award in 1999.
significance: Expanded jazz's emotional palette with its impressionistic approach. It demonstrated that jazz could be atmospheric and accessible while remaining harmonically sophisticated.

title: Music Matador
label: Blue Note
description: Matador is an album by American jazz guitarist Grant Green. It contains performances recorded in 1964 but not released on the Japanese Blue Note label until 1979. It features Green with pianist McCoy Tyner and drummer Elvin Jones, and bassist Bob Cranshaw. The album was finally reissued in the U.S. on CD in 1990 with one bonus track. It was also reissued on vinyl in 2010, with a different cover.
significance: Demonstrated Green's versatility beyond the soul jazz he became known for. The Latin-jazz fusion predated later trends by decades.

title: Pastel Blues
label: Philips
description: Pastel Blues is a studio album by American singer Nina Simone, released in October 1, 1965, by Philips Records.
significance: Contains some of Simone's most powerful performances. 'Sinnerman' became a anthem of spiritual struggle and release.
keyTracks: Tell Me More and More and Then Some

title: Point of Departure
label: Blue Note
description: Point of Departure is a studio album by American jazz pianist and composer Andrew Hill, recorded in 1964 and released in 1965 on the Blue Note label. It features Hill in a sextet with alto saxophonist Eric Dolphy, tenor saxophonist Joe Henderson, trumpeter Kenny Dorham, bassist Richard Davis and drummer Tony Williams.
significance: Proved that avant-garde jazz could be structured and accessible. Hill's unique harmonic language influenced generations.

title: Ray Brown / Milt Jackson
label: Verve
description: Goodbye is an album by vibraphonist Milt Jackson recorded in 1973 and released on the CTI label.
significance: Musician player Milt Jackson's big band sensibility is on display in this 1965 session, a document of the hard bop era.

title: Solo Monk
label: Columbia
description: Solo Monk (1965) is the fifth studio album Thelonious Monk recorded for Columbia Records, and his eighth overall for that label. The album is composed of solo piano work by Monk. The AllMusic review by Thom Jurek states, "This is perhaps the solo piano record to have by Monk". In addition to various vinyl and CD issues, Sony Music Enterprises issued an SACD in Japan.
significance: A hard bop entry from 1965 by Thelonious Monk, documenting the breadth of the hard bop period.

title: Some Other Stuff
label: Blue Note
description: Some Other Stuff is the second album by American trombonist Grachan Moncur III, recorded on July 6, 1964 and released on Blue Note early the following year. The quintet features saxophonist Wayne Shorter, and rhythm section Herbie Hancock, Cecil McBee and Tony Williams.
significance: Captures the avant-garde side of musicians better known for their mainstream work. Hancock and Shorter stretch into territory they rarely explored on record.

title: Song for My Father
label: Blue Note
description: Song for My Father is a 1965 album by the Horace Silver Quintet, released on the Blue Note label in 1965. The album was inspired by a trip that Silver had made to Brazil. The cover artwork features a photograph of Silver's father, John Tavares Silver, to whom the title composition was dedicated. "My mother was of Irish and Negro descent, my father of Portuguese origin," Silver recalls in the liner notes: "He was born on the island of Maio, one of the Cape Verde Islands."
significance: The title track's riff was later sampled by Steely Dan for 'Rikki Don't Lose That Number.' Silver's funky hard bop style influenced generations.

title: Soul Sauce
label: Verve
description: Vibraphonist Cal Tjader's biggest commercial success builds on the irresistible Dizzy Gillespie-penned title track. Tjader's shimmering vibes float over Latin percussion grooves with an effortless cool that made him the most popular Latin jazz artist of the 1960s.
significance: Brought Latin jazz to its widest audience yet and established the boogaloo groove that would dominate mid-60s Latin music. Tjader's accessible, swinging approach introduced countless listeners to Afro-Cuban rhythms.

title: Spiritual Unity
label: ESP-Disk
description: Spiritual Unity is a studio album by American free jazz saxophonist Albert Ayler. It was recorded on July 10, 1964 in New York City, and features bassist Gary Peacock and drummer Sunny Murray. It was the first album recorded for Bernard Stollman's ESP-Disk label, and it brought Ayler to international attention as it was so "shockingly different". At the same time, it transformed ESP-Disk into "a major source for avant-garde jazz". A 5-star review in AllMusic called it a "landmark recording that's essential to any basic understanding of free jazz", "the album that pushed Albert Ayler to the forefront of jazz's avant-garde...
significance: Influenced countless free jazz musicians and inspired John Coltrane's late period explorations.

title: The Heliocentric Worlds of Sun Ra, Volume 1
label: ESP‐Disk’
description: The Heliocentric Worlds of Sun Ra, Volume One is a 1965 album by the jazz musician Sun Ra. The back cover describes it as an "album of compositions and arrangements by Sun Ra played by Sun Ra and his Solar Arkestra".
significance: The back cover describes it as an "album of compositions and arrangements by Sun Ra played by Sun Ra and his Solar Arkestra".

title: A Sign of the Times
label: World Pacific
description: A Sign of the Times is an album by American jazz guitarist Joe Pass that was released in 1965 by World Pacific Records. It includes orchestrated versions of pop hits. The title track was a hit for Petula Clark. The album was reissued on CD with Simplicity in 2002 by Euphoria.
significance: It includes orchestrated versions of pop hits.

title: Ascension
label: Impulse!
description: Ascension is a studio album by the jazz musician and composer John Coltrane. It was released through Impulse! Records in February 1966. It is considered a watershed in Coltrane's work, with the albums recorded before it being more conventional in structure and the albums recorded after it being looser, free jazz inspired works. In addition, it signaled Coltrane's interest in moving away from the quartet format. AllMusic called it "the single recording that placed John Coltrane firmly into the avant-garde". It was recorded at the Van Gelder Studio in June 1965.
significance: Pushed free jazz to its limits. Its intensity and spirituality influenced noise music, metal, and avant-garde rock.

title: Complete Communion
label: Blue Note
description: Complete Communion is a 1966 album by American jazz composer Don Cherry, his debut as a bandleader and his first release on Blue Note Records.
significance: Pointed toward the world music direction Cherry would pursue. The suite structure and collective interplay influenced the AACM and European free jazz scenes.

title: Ella at Duke’s Place
label: Verve
description:
Ella at Duke's Place is a 1965 studio album by Ella Fitzgerald and Duke Ellington, accompanied by his Orchestra. While it was the second studio album made by Fitzgerald and Ellington, following the 1957 song book recording, a live double album Ella and Duke at the Cote D'Azur was recorded in 1966.
Ella at Duke's Place was nominated for Best Female Pop Vocal Performance at the 1967 Grammy Awards.
significance: Ella at Duke's Place was nominated for Best Female Pop Vocal Performance at the 1967 Grammy Awards.
keyTracks: A Flower Is a Lovesome Thing

title: Ella at Duke’s Place
label: Verve
description: Duke Ellington's piano artistry is on full display on Ella at Duke’s Place, a 1966 Verve release steeped in jazz. The set list features 'Something to Live For' and 'A Flower Is a Lovesome Thing' among its standout moments.
significance: The set list features 'Something to Live For' and 'A Flower Is a Lovesome Thing' among its standout moments.
keyTracks: A Flower Is a Lovesome Thing

title: Globe Unity
label: SABA
description: German pianist Alexander von Schlippenbach assembled fourteen musicians for this explosive big band session that merged post-serial composition with collective free improvisation. The Globe Unity Orchestra became one of the most important large ensembles in European free music.
significance: Founded the European free jazz big band tradition. The Globe Unity Orchestra proved that large-scale free improvisation could be as powerful and structured as composed music.

title: Happenings
label: Blue Note
description: Vibraphonist Bobby Hutcherson's masterpiece with pianist Herbie Hancock, bassist Bob Cranshaw, and drummer Joe Chambers. The music exists in a luminous space between hard bop and the avant-garde, with Hutcherson's shimmering vibes creating kaleidoscopic textures.
significance: Established Hutcherson as the most important vibraphonist since Milt Jackson. The album's blend of lyricism and harmonic daring made it a touchstone for modern post-bop.

title: Here and There
label: Prestige
description: Here and There is a jazz album by multi-instrumentalist Eric Dolphy. It was originally released in 1966 on the Prestige label as PRLP 7382. It contains tracks recorded on three separate dates, in different locations. Mal Waldron's "Status Seeking" and Dolphy's solo bass clarinet version of Billie Holiday's "God Bless the Child" were recorded on July 16, 1961, at the Five Spot in New York City as part of the concert that was documented on the At the Five Spot recordings. "April Fool" and "G.W.
significance: It was originally released in 1966 on the Prestige label as PRLP 7382.

title: Indo-Jazz Suite
label: Columbia
description: Joe Harriott's visionary collaboration with Indian violinist John Mayer was the first serious attempt to fuse jazz improvisation with Indian classical music. The double quintet—five jazz musicians and five Indian classical musicians—creates a genuine dialogue between two great improvisational traditions.
significance: Predated the Indo-jazz experiments of John McLaughlin and others by several years. Harriott and Mayer's respectful, equal partnership between traditions remains a model for cross-cultural musical collaboration.

title: Inside Basie Outside
label: VSP
description: A swing outing from 1966, Inside Basie Outside features pianist Count Basie recording for VSP. The program includes "Swingin' at Newport", "One O'Clock Jump", and "Basie's Back in Town".
significance: The program includes "Swingin' at Newport", "One O'Clock Jump", and "Basie's Back in Town".

title: Meditations
label: Impulse!
description: Meditations is a 1966 album by John Coltrane. The album was considered the "spiritual follow-up to A Love Supreme." It features Coltrane and Pharoah Sanders as soloists, both playing tenor saxophones. This was the last Coltrane recording to feature his classic quartet lineup of himself, bassist Jimmy Garrison, drummer Elvin Jones and pianist McCoy Tyner, as both Jones and Tyner would quit the band by early 1966. Sanders, Ali, Garrison and Coltrane's wife Alice would comprise his next group.
significance: Pushed Coltrane's spiritual jazz to its extreme. Its intensity influenced noise music and spiritual jazz revivals.
keyTracks: The Father and the Son and the Holy Ghost

title: Mercy, Mercy, Mercy!
label: Capitol
description: Mercy, Mercy, Mercy! Live at "The Club" is a 1967 live in-studio album by The Cannonball Adderley Quintet, the jazz group formed by musician Cannonball Adderley. It received the Grammy Award for Best Instrumental Jazz Performance – Group or Soloist with Group in 1967, and was added to the Grammy Hall of Fame in 2021.
significance: The title track became a crossover hit, proving jazz could reach mainstream audiences while maintaining integrity.

title: Mode for Joe
label: Blue Note
description: Mode for Joe is the fifth studio album by American jazz saxophonist Joe Henderson, recorded and released in 1966. Featuring Henderson with a larger than usual ensemble consisting of trumpeter Lee Morgan, trombonist Curtis Fuller, vibraphonist Bobby Hutcherson, pianist Cedar Walton, bassist Ron Carter and drummer Joe Chambers, it was Henderson’s last Blue Note recording as leader until the live albums The State of the Tenor, Vols. 1 & 2 almost 20 years later.
significance: Established Henderson as a major voice on tenor saxophone. His blend of inside and outside playing was influential.

title: Music on My Mind
label: MPS
description: William Henry Joseph Bonaparte Bertholf Smith, nicknamed "the Lion", was an American jazz and stride pianist.
significance: A early jazz entry from 1966 by Willie "the Lion" Smith, documenting the breadth of the free jazz period.

title: New York Eye and Ear Control
label: ESP-Disk
description: New York Eye and Ear Control is an album of group improvisations recorded in July 1964 by an augmented version of Albert Ayler's group to provide the soundtrack for Michael Snow's film of the same name.
significance: Ayler's raw, primal saxophone sound influenced punk, noise, and free improvisation worldwide.

title: Sayin' Somethin'
label: Unknown
description: Sayin' Somethin' is an album by jazz cornetist Nat Adderley, released on the Atlantic label and featuring four performances by Adderley with an 11-piece orchestra and four by Adderley's Quintet with Joe Henderson, Herbie Hancock, Bob Cranshaw, and Roy McCurdy.
significance: From the free jazz era, when musicians abandoned predetermined structures for collective expression, this album positions Nat Adderley within the bebop conversation of the time.

title: Sound
label: Delmark
description: The first recording by the Association for the Advancement of Creative Musicians (AACM), featuring the Roscoe Mitchell Sextet with Lester Bowie and Malachi Favors. The album alternates between dense collective improvisation and radical silence, using 'little instruments' and space in revolutionary ways.
significance: The founding document of the AACM and Chicago's creative music scene. This album's radical approach to sound, silence, and texture changed the course of experimental music and spawned the Art Ensemble of Chicago.

title: Speak No Evil
label: Blue Note
description: Speak No Evil is the sixth album by Wayne Shorter. It was released in June 1966 by Blue Note Records. The music combines elements of hard bop and modal jazz, and features Shorter on tenor saxophone, trumpeter Freddie Hubbard, pianist Herbie Hancock, bassist Ron Carter and drummer Elvin Jones. The cover photo is of Shorter's first wife, Teruko (Irene) Nakagami, whom he met in 1961.
significance: Established Shorter as one of jazz's most important composers. His mysterious, cinematic style influenced countless musicians.

title: Tequila
label: Verve
description: Tequila is an album recorded by the jazz guitarist Wes Montgomery, released in 1966.
significance: Documents guitarist Wes Montgomery's cool jazz work for Verve, a 1966 session reflecting the free jazz period.
keyTracks: What the World Needs Now Is Love

title: Unit Structures
label: Blue Note
description: Unit Structures is a studio album by American jazz pianist Cecil Taylor, released in October 1966 by Blue Note Records.
significance: Established Taylor as free jazz's most uncompromising voice. His percussive piano style influenced countless avant-garde musicians.

title: AMMMusic
label: Elektra
description: The London-based collective AMM—featuring guitarist Keith Rowe, percussionist Eddie Prevost, and others—created one of the most radical recordings of the 1960s. AMMMusic abandons melody, harmony, and rhythm entirely in favor of pure sound exploration, predating ambient and noise music by decades.
significance: A foundational document of free improvisation distinct from free jazz. AMM's approach influenced Brian Eno, Cornelius Cardew, and the entire experimental music tradition.
keyTracks: Later During a Flaming Riviera Sunset

title: Alligator Bogaloo
label: Blue Note
description: Alto saxophonist Lou Donaldson's funkiest album rides a deep boogaloo groove courtesy of organist Lonnie Smith and drummer Leo Morris (Idris Muhammad). The music is pure dancefloor joy, with Donaldson's bluesy alto cutting through the infectious rhythms.
significance: One of the biggest sellers in Blue Note history and a blueprint for jazz-funk. The album proved that jazz could move bodies without sacrificing improvisational quality, and it remains a DJ staple.

title: Conquistador!
label: Blue Note
description: Conquistador! is a 1968 studio album recorded in 1966 by free jazz pianist Cecil Taylor, released by Blue Note Records.
significance: is a 1968 studio album recorded in 1966 by free jazz pianist Cecil Taylor, released by Blue Note Records.

title: Contours
label: Blue Note
description: Contours is the second album by American saxophonist Sam Rivers, recorded in 1965 and released on the Blue Note label. The CD reissue contains an alternate take as a bonus track.
significance: Showed that the avant-garde could swing. Rivers's ability to move fluidly between inside and outside playing became a template for future improvisers.

title: El Hombre
label: Prestige
description: Pat Martino's debut as a leader, recorded when he was just 22, already displays the linear, horn-like approach that would make him one of jazz guitar's most distinctive voices. With Trudy Pitts on organ and Danny Turner on flute, the album burns with youthful intensity.
significance: Announced Martino as a major new voice in jazz guitar. His incredibly fluid, rapid-fire single-note lines and rhythmic drive set a new technical standard for the instrument.

title: Ella Fitzgerald’s Christmas
label: Capitol Records
description: Composer player Ella Fitzgerald's 1967 album Ella Fitzgerald’s Christmas draws from the free jazz tradition, released on Capitol Records. Standouts include "O Holy Night - Remastered 2006" and "It Came Upon a Midnight Clear - Remastered 2006".
significance: She was noted for her purity of tone, impeccable diction, phrasing, timing, intonation, absolute pitch, and a "horn-like" improvisational ability, particularly in her scat singing.
keyTracks: It Came Upon a Midnight Clear - Remastered 2006
Hark! The Herald Angels Sing - Remastered 2006

title: Kulu Sé Mama
label: Impulse!
description: Kulu Sé Mama is a free jazz album by John Coltrane. Recorded during 1965, it was released in January 1967 as Impulse! A-9106, and was the last album released during Coltrane's lifetime.
significance: Documents Coltrane's embrace of African spiritual traditions. The album's mix of meditative beauty and ecstatic release captures his late-period range.

title: Mama Too Tight
label: Impulse!
description: Mama Too Tight is an album by Archie Shepp released on Impulse! Records in 1967. The album contains tracks recorded by Shepp, trumpeter Tommy Turrentine, trombonists Grachan Moncur III and Roswell Rudd, tuba player Howard Johnson, clarinetist Perry Robinson, bassist Charlie Haden and drummer Beaver Harris in August 1966.
significance: Demonstrated that free jazz could embrace jazz history rather than reject it. Shepp's mix of Ellington, blues, and the avant-garde created a template for musicians like David Murray.

title: Miles Smiles
label: Unknown
description: Miles Smiles is an album by American jazz musician Miles Davis, released on February 16, 1967, by Columbia Records. It was recorded by Davis and his second quintet at Columbia 30th Street Studio in New York City on October 24 and October 25, 1966. It is the second of six albums recorded by Davis' second great quintet, which featured tenor saxophonist Wayne Shorter, pianist Herbie Hancock, bassist Ron Carter, and drummer Tony Williams.
significance: It was recorded by Davis and his second quintet at Columbia 30th Street Studio in New York City on October 24 and October 25, 1966.

title: Mr. Jelly Lord
label: RCA Victor
description: With Mr. Jelly Lord (1967), pianist Jelly Roll Morton delivers a free jazz program for the RCA Victor label. The date includes "Burrnin' The Iceburg" and "Mr. Jelly Lord". The album comes from the free jazz era, when musicians abandoned predetermined structures for collective expression.
significance: Jelly Lord (1967), pianist Jelly Roll Morton delivers a free jazz program for the RCA Victor label.
keyTracks: When They Get Lovin' They's Gone

title: Mr. Shing-A-Ling
label: Blue Note
description: Mr. Shing-a-Ling is an album by American jazz saxophonist Lou Donaldson, recorded on October 27, 1967 and released on Blue Note in January 1968. The quintet features trumpeter Blue Mitchell, organist Lonnie Smith, guitarist Jimmy Ponder, and drummer Idris Muhammad.
significance: Shing-a-Ling is an album by American jazz saxophonist Lou Donaldson, recorded on October 27, 1967 and released on Blue Note in January 1968.

title: One Flight Up
label: Blue Note
description: One Flight Up is an album by American jazz saxophonist Dexter Gordon recorded on June 2, 1964, in Paris and released on Blue Note the following year. The session featured Donald Byrd on trumpet, pianist Kenny Drew, bassist Niels-Henning Ørsted Pedersen, and drummer Art Taylor.
significance: The session featured Donald Byrd on trumpet, pianist Kenny Drew, bassist Niels-Henning Ørsted Pedersen, and drummer Art Taylor.

title: Straight, No Chaser
label: Columbia
description: Straight, No Chaser is the sixth studio album Thelonious Monk recorded for Columbia Records, released in 1967. The album was reissued on CD in 1996, including restored versions of previously abridged performances and three additional tracks.
significance: Captures Monk's mature quartet in peak form. His influence on jazz harmony and melody is fully displayed.

title: Street of Dreams
label: Blue Note
description: Street of Dreams is an album by American jazz guitarist Grant Green featuring performances recorded in 1964 and released on the Blue Note label. The street signs featured on the cover photo are at an actual street corner in the North Beach neighborhood of San Francisco. Green is heard with organist Larry Young, vibraphonist Bobby Hutcherson and drummer Elvin Jones.
significance: Captures Green at his most relaxed and expressive. His single-note lines tell stories with the directness of a great blues singer.

title: Tauhid
label: Impulse!
description: Pharoah Sanders' debut as a leader after his transformative work with John Coltrane. Tauhid (the Islamic concept of divine unity) channels the energy of Coltrane's late period into Sanders' own fierce, spiritual vision, blending extended techniques with Middle Eastern and African influences.
significance: Established Sanders as a major voice in spiritual jazz, separate from Coltrane's shadow. The album's blend of free improvisation and world music laid the groundwork for the entire spiritual jazz movement.

title: The Far East Suite
label: Victor
description: Far East Suite is a 1967 concept album by American jazz musician Duke Ellington, inspired by his group's tour of Asia. Ellington and longtime collaborator Billy Strayhorn wrote the compositions.
significance: Ellington and longtime collaborator Billy Strayhorn wrote the compositions.

title: The Magic of Ju-Ju
label: Impulse!
description: The Magic of Ju-Ju is an album by Archie Shepp released on Impulse! Records in May 1968.
significance: One of the first jazz albums to explicitly draw on African musical and spiritual traditions. Its Afrocentric approach influenced the Black Arts Movement and later Afrocentric jazz.
keyTracks: You're What This Day Is All About

title: The Olatunji Concert: The Last Live Recording
label: Impulse!
description: The Olatunji Concert: The Last Live Recording is the Impulse! Records-released final live recording of saxophonist John Coltrane, recorded April 23, 1967, at the Olatunji Center of African Culture in New York and released on Compact Disc in 2001. The album consists of two songs—"Ogunde", which Coltrane also recorded for his final approved album, Expression, and an especially free-form "My Favorite Things", which Coltrane had performed live regularly since 1960. The recording was made for broadcast on Billy Taylor's local radio station, WLIB.
significance: The most extreme document of Coltrane's final period. Its uncompromising intensity divided listeners but inspired the punk rock ethos of total commitment.

title: The Real McCoy
label: Blue Note
description: The Real McCoy is the seventh album by jazz pianist McCoy Tyner and his first released on the Blue Note label. It was recorded on April 21, 1967, two years after Tyner's departure from the John Coltrane Quartet and during a difficult period in which Tyner considered leaving jazz and taking a day job as a cab driver. It features performances by Tyner with tenor saxophonist Joe Henderson, bassist Ron Carter, and former Coltrane Quartet drummer Elvin Jones. Producer Alfred Lion recalls the recording session as a "pure jazz session.
significance: Established Tyner as a major leader and composer. His quartets throughout the late 1960s defined the post-Coltrane aesthetic.

title: Afro-Harping
label: Cadet
description: Afro-Harping is an album by jazz harpist Dorothy Ashby recorded in 1968 and released on the Cadet label.
significance: Created an Afrocentric soul jazz sound that predated similar experiments by decades. Recently rediscovered by crate diggers and sampled extensively.

title: Clarinet Concertos Nos. 1 And 2
label: RCA Red Seal
description: Benny Goodman's Clarinet Concertos Nos. 1 And 2, cut for RCA Red Seal in 1968, stands as a distinguished entry in the swing canon. The set list features 'Concerto for Clarinet and Orchestra no. 1 in F minor, op. 73: I. Allegro' and 'Concerto for Clarinet and Orchestra no. 1 in F minor, op. 73: II. Adagio ma non troppo' among its standout moments.
significance: 1 And 2, cut for RCA Red Seal in 1968, stands as a distinguished entry in the swing canon.
keyTracks: Concerto for Clarinet and Orchestra no. 1 in F minor, op. 73: I. Allegro
Concerto for Clarinet and Orchestra no. 1 in F minor, op. 73: II. Adagio ma non troppo
Concerto for Clarinet and Orchestra no. 1 in F minor, op. 73: III. Rondo. Allegretto
Concerto for Clarinet and Orchestra no. 2 in E-flat major, op. 74: I. Allegro
Concerto for Clarinet and Orchestra no. 2 in E-flat major, op. 74: II. Andante con moto

title: Hugh Masekela's Grazin'
label: Uni
description: "Grazing in the Grass" is an instrumental composed by Philemon Hou and first recorded by the South African trumpeter Hugh Masekela. Released in the United States as a single in 1968, it followed United States trumpeter Herb Alpert's vocal performance of "This Guy's in Love with You" to the top spot on the Hot 100 chart, ranking it as the 18th biggest hit of the year. The song also reached No. 15 Adult Contemporary. Masekela included the song in his albums Grazing in the Grass: The Best of Hugh Masekela (2001), Still Grazing (2004), and Live at the Market Theatre (2006).
significance: The biggest crossover hit in African jazz history. Masekela became an ambassador for South African music worldwide.
keyTracks: Do Me So La So So

title: Levels and Degrees of Light
label: Delmark
description: The AACM's founding father delivers a visionary album that encompasses free jazz, poetry, vocal chants, and extended instrumental techniques. With a cast including Anthony Braxton and Leroy Jenkins, Abrams creates a total music that transcends genre categories entirely.
significance: Embodied the AACM's philosophy of 'Great Black Music—Ancient to the Future.' Abrams' inclusive, non-hierarchical approach to composition and improvisation mentored an entire generation of creative musicians.

title: Machine Gun
label: BRÖ
description: Machine Gun is the second album by German avant-garde jazz saxophonist Peter Brötzmann, originally released on his BRÖ label in 1968.
significance: Defined European free jazz as distinct from its American counterpart—rawer, more industrial, less concerned with swing. It remains a rite of passage for adventurous listeners.

title: Mexican Green
label: Fontana
description: Britain's greatest tenor saxophonist delivers a stunning late-career statement with a quintet featuring vibraphonist Mike Pyne. Hayes' torrential technique and harmonic sophistication rivaled any American hard bop player, and his original compositions bristle with angular energy.
significance: The finest document of Tubby Hayes' mature artistry before his tragically early death at 38. The album proves that British jazz could stand alongside the best American hard bop on equal terms.

title: Now He Sings, Now He Sobs
label: Solid State
description: Now He Sings, Now He Sobs is the second studio album by Chick Corea, released in December 1968 on Solid State Records. It features Corea in a trio with bassist Miroslav Vitouš and drummer Roy Haynes. In 1988 it was reissued on CD by Blue Note with eight bonus tracks recorded at the same sessions.
significance: Established Corea as a major voice in jazz piano. The album's energy and sophistication influenced generations.
keyTracks: Now He Sings, Now He Sobs

title: Piano Starts Here
label: Columbia
description: With Piano Starts Here (1968), pianist Art Tatum delivers a swing program for the Columbia label. Among the tracks are "Tea for Two" and "St. Louis Blues". The album comes from the big band era when jazz was America's popular music, driven by dancing and radio.
significance: Tatum also extended jazz piano's vocabulary and boundaries far beyond his initial stride influences, and established new ground through innovative use of reharmonization, voicing, and bitonality.

title: Selflessness, Featuring My Favorite Things
label: impulse!
description: Selflessness Featuring My Favorite Things is a posthumous album by jazz musician John Coltrane, released in 1969. The album juxtaposes two tracks recorded live at the 1963 Newport Jazz Festival with a single track ("Selflessness") recorded in a studio in Los Angeles in 1965.
significance: The album juxtaposes two tracks recorded live at the 1963 Newport Jazz Festival with a single track ("Selflessness") recorded in a studio in Los Angeles in 1965.
keyTracks: I Want To Talk About You

title: Speak Like a Child
label: Blue Note
description: Speak Like a Child, the sixth album by American jazz pianist Herbie Hancock, which was recorded and released by Blue Note Records in 1968, features Thad Jones's (uncredited) arrangements of Hancock's compositions for an unusual front line of Jerry Dodgion on alto flute, Peter Phillips on bass trombone, and Thad Jones on flugelhorn.
significance: From the free jazz era, when musicians abandoned predetermined structures for collective expression, this album positions Herbie Hancock within the hard bop conversation of the time.

title: The Piano Soul of Nat King Cole
label: Pickwick/33 Records
description: Nathaniel Adams Coles, known professionally as Nat King Cole, was an American singer, jazz pianist, and actor. Cole's career as a jazz and pop vocalist started in the late 1930s and spanned almost three decades where he found success and recorded over 100 songs that became hits on the pop charts.
significance: Cole's career as a jazz and pop vocalist started in the late 1930s and spanned almost three decades where he found success and recorded over 100 songs that became hits on the pop charts.

title: Time for Tyner
label: Blue Note
description: Time for Tyner is the ninth album as a leader by jazz pianist McCoy Tyner and his third released on the Blue Note label. It was recorded in May 1968 and features performances by Tyner with vibraphonist Bobby Hutcherson, bassist Herbie Lewis, and drummer Freddie Waits.
significance: It was recorded in May 1968 and features performances by Tyner with vibraphonist Bobby Hutcherson, bassist Herbie Lewis, and drummer Freddie Waits.
keyTracks: I Didn’t Know What Time It Was
The Surrey With the Fringe on Top

title: Total Eclipse
label: Blue Note
description: Total Eclipse is an album by jazz vibraphonist Bobby Hutcherson, released on the Blue Note label in 1969. It features Hutcherson's first recordings with saxophonist Harold Land, who would become a regular collaborator with Hutcherson throughout the early 1970s. Four of the five tracks are Hutcherson compositions, the exception being Chick Corea's "Matrix".
significance: Pushed the vibraphone beyond its cool jazz associations into darker, more mysterious territory. A cult favorite among musicians and collectors.

title: Black Woman
label: Vortex
description: Black Woman is the debut album by American jazz guitarist Sonny Sharrock, recorded in 1969 and released on the Vortex label. In 2000, it was reissued by the Collectables label, paired with the Wayne Henderson album People Get Ready.
significance: Invented free jazz guitar. Sharrock's noise-rock approach influenced no wave, hardcore punk, and metal decades later.
keyTracks: Portrait of Linda in Three Colors

title: For Alto
label: Delmark
description: The first full-length solo saxophone recording in jazz history, Anthony Braxton's For Alto is an exhaustive exploration of the alto saxophone's sonic possibilities. Each piece is dedicated to a different musician, from John Cage to Cecil Taylor, mapping the terrain between jazz and contemporary classical music.
significance: A watershed moment in jazz history that opened up the solo saxophone recital as a viable format. Braxton's systematic yet deeply expressive approach influenced every solo saxophonist who followed.

title: Free at Last
label: ECM
description: One of ECM's very first releases, recorded by Mal Waldron's European-based trio. After his devastating nervous breakdown and memory loss in the mid-1960s, Waldron rebuilt his style into something starker and more obsessively repetitive, creating hypnotic vamps that build to overwhelming intensity.
significance: Among the earliest ECM recordings and a powerful document of artistic rebirth. Waldron's post-breakdown style—minimal, insistent, deeply emotional—anticipated minimalism and influenced a generation of European pianists.

title: Gin and Orange
label: Cadet Records
description: Gin and Orange is a 1969 album by organist Brother Jack McDuff recorded both live and in the studio which was his third release on the Cadet label.
significance: A 1969 jazz-funk date for Cadet Records that documents Brother Jack McDuff's ongoing work in the idiom.

title: In a Silent Way
label: Columbia
description: In a Silent Way is a studio album by the American jazz trumpeter, composer, and bandleader Miles Davis, released on July 30, 1969, on Columbia Records. Produced by Teo Macero, the album was recorded in one session date on February 18, 1969, at CBS 30th Street Studio in New York City. Macero edited and arranged Davis's recordings from the session to produce the album. Marking the beginning of his "electric" period, In a Silent Way has been regarded by music writers as Davis's first fusion recording, following a stylistic shift toward the genre in his previous records and live performances.
significance: Created a new jazz aesthetic: spacious, ambient, and hypnotic. It paved the way for Bitches Brew and all of fusion.
keyTracks: In a Silent Way/It's About That Time

title: Jewels of Thought
label: Impulse!
description: Jewels of Thought is an album by the American jazz saxophonist Pharoah Sanders. It was recorded at Plaza Sound Studios in New York City on October 20, 1969, and was released on Impulse! Records in the same year. The 1998 reissue merged "Sun In Aquarius" into one 27-minute-long track.
significance: Demonstrates the full range of Sanders's spiritual jazz vision—from ecstatic screaming to tender meditation. A touchstone for the contemporary spiritual jazz revival.

title: Karma
label: Impulse!
description: Karma is a jazz recording by the American tenor saxophonist Pharoah Sanders, released in May 1969 on the Impulse! label, with catalog number AS 9181. A pioneering work of the spiritual jazz style, it has become Sanders' most popular and critically acclaimed album, with Pitchfork ranking it number 53 on its list of the greatest albums of the 1960s.
significance: The most commercially successful spiritual jazz album, proving that challenging music could move mainstream audiences. Its message of peace and unity resonated during the tumultuous late 1960s.
keyTracks: The Creator Has a Master Plan

title: Key for Two
label: BYG Records
description: Released on BYG Records in 1969, Key for Two is a compelling contemporary jazz statement by Hampton Hawes. Among its key performances are 'Key for Two' and 'Stella by Starlight'.
significance: Among its key performances are 'Key for Two' and 'Stella by Starlight'.
keyTracks: Spring Can Really Hang You Up the Most

title: Monk’s Blues
label: CBS
description: Monk's Dream is an album by jazz pianist Thelonious Monk, released by Columbia Records in January 1963. It was Monk's first album for Columbia following his five-year recording period with Riverside Records.
significance: It was Monk's first album for Columbia following his five-year recording period with Riverside Records.

title: Power to the People
label: Milestone
description: Henderson's socially conscious masterwork captures the revolutionary spirit of the late 1960s. Featuring Herbie Hancock, Ron Carter, and Jack DeJohnette, the album balances political urgency with Henderson's characteristically sophisticated harmonic language.
significance: Demonstrated that hard bop could engage with the political moment without abandoning musical sophistication. Henderson's warm yet adventurous tone bridged the gap between mainstream and avant-garde jazz.

title: Spirits Known and Unknown
label: Flying Dutchman
description: Leon Thomas' extraordinary debut showcases his unique yodeling vocal technique alongside Pharoah Sanders and a stellar band. The album bridges African vocal traditions, free jazz, and soul music in ways that had never been attempted before.
significance: Introduced Thomas' revolutionary vocal approach to a wide audience. His technique of channeling pygmy yodeling through jazz singing created an entirely new vocabulary for vocal improvisation.
keyTracks: The Creator Has a Master Plan

title: Super Nova
label: Blue Note
description: Super Nova is the twelfth album by Wayne Shorter, recorded in 1969 and released on the Blue Note label. The album features five originals by Shorter and an arrangement of "Dindi" by Antônio Carlos Jobim. "Water Babies", "Capricorn" and "Sweet Pea" were originally recorded in 1967 during sessions with Miles Davis that would eventually be released in 1976 as the album Water Babies.
significance: Bridged Shorter's Blue Note work with his later Weather Report direction. Its textural approach anticipated ambient and world music fusions.

title: The New Don Ellis Band Goes Underground
label: Columbia
description: Don Ellis at Fillmore is a live double-LP by big band leader Don Ellis which was released in 1970.
significance: Later in his life he worked as a film composer, contributing a score to 1971's The French Connection and 1973's The Seven-Ups.

title: To Love Somebody
label: RCA
description: To Love Somebody is an album by jazz singer-songwriter/pianist Nina Simone. Primarily a covers album, it was released as quickly as possible to prolong the unexpected success of 'Nuff Said! The title is taken from the Bee Gees song "To Love Somebody"; her cover of the song became her second British hit single after "Ain't Got No, I Got Life".
significance: Primarily a covers album, it was released as quickly as possible to prolong the unexpected success of 'Nuff Said!
keyTracks: Turn! Turn! Turn! (To Everything There Is a Season)

title: Alive!
label: Blue Note
description: Guitarist Grant Green's electrifying live album captures him at the Cliche Lounge in Newark with a smoking organ combo. His single-note lines dance over funky grooves with a rhythmic intensity that bridges jazz and R&B. The extended takes allow Green to build to ecstatic peaks.
significance: Captured Green at his most soulful and funky, proving him one of the greatest rhythm guitarists in jazz. The album became hugely popular with DJs and hip-hop producers, making Green a posthumous icon.
keyTracks: Let the Music Take Your Mind

title: Bitches Brew
label: Columbia
description: A double album that shattered the boundaries between jazz and rock, recorded in August 1969 with an extraordinary ensemble including Wayne Shorter, Chick Corea, Joe Zawinul, John McLaughlin, Dave Holland, and multiple drummers. Producer Teo Macero's revolutionary editing techniques spliced and layered performances into a dense, otherworldly soundscape. The sessions were largely improvised around sketches Miles provided, creating an organic yet alien sonic world. Mati Klarwein's striking Afrofuturist cover art perfectly captured the album's revolutionary spirit.
significance: Launched the jazz fusion movement and became the first jazz album to sell 500,000 copies in its first year. Its studio-as-instrument approach pioneered production techniques still used today. The album influenced not just fusion but progressive rock, ambient music, and electronic music. Musicians from Santana to Radiohead cite it as foundational.

title: Deaf, Dumb, Blind: Summun, Bukmun, Umyun
label: impulse!
description: Deaf Dumb Blind (Summun Bukmun Umyun) is an album by the American jazz saxophonist Pharoah Sanders. It was recorded at A & R Studios in New York City on July 1, 1970, and released on Impulse! Records in the same year. The album's title is bilingual: "Summun Bukmun Umyun" is Arabic for "Deaf Dumb Blind".
significance: It was recorded at A & R Studios in New York City on July 1, 1970, and released on Impulse!
keyTracks: Let Us Go Into the House of the Lord

title: Extensions
label: Blue Note
description: Extensions is the eleventh album by jazz pianist McCoy Tyner released on the Blue Note label. It was recorded on February 9, 1970, but not released until January 1973. It has performances by Tyner with alto saxophonist Gary Bartz, tenor saxophonist Wayne Shorter, bassist Ron Carter, drummer Elvin Jones, and features Alice Coltrane playing harp on three of the four tracks.
significance: Showed Tyner's growth beyond his Coltrane Quartet years. His percussive left hand technique influenced generations.

title: Jazz in Africa
label: RCA
description: Chris McGregor's Brotherhood of Breath is the debut album by South African pianist and composer Chris McGregor's big band of the same name. Produced by Joe Boyd, it was recorded in 1970, and was issued on LP by the short-lived Neon imprint of RCA Records in 1971 as the label's second release. In 2007, it was reissued on CD by Fledg'ling Records.
significance: Documented the South African jazz diaspora. McGregor's multi-racial band was both musical statement and political act against apartheid.

title: Les Stances à Sophie
label: Pathé
description: The Art Ensemble of Chicago is an avant-garde jazz group that grew out of the Association for the Advancement of Creative Musicians (AACM) in the late 1960s. The ensemble integrates many jazz styles and plays many instruments, including "little instruments": bells, bicycle horns, birthday party noisemakers, wind chimes, and various forms of percussion. The musicians would wear costumes and face paint while performing. These characteristics combined to make the ensemble's performances both aural and visual. While playing in Europe in 1969, five hundred instruments were used.
significance: Showed the Art Ensemble's range and their ability to work in commercial contexts without compromising. 'Theme de Yoyo' became a rare avant-garde dance floor hit.
keyTracks: Variations sur un Theme de Monteverdi

title: Liberation Music Orchestra
label: Impulse!
description: Bassist Charlie Haden assembled an extraordinary ensemble including Don Cherry, Dewey Redman, and Carla Bley to create politically engaged jazz that drew on Spanish Civil War songs and protest music. Bley's arrangements transform folk melodies into sprawling free jazz odysseys.
significance: One of the most powerful political statements in jazz history. Haden's concept of the Liberation Music Orchestra—music as a weapon against injustice—has been revived repeatedly and remains a model for politically committed jazz.

title: Live at the Lighthouse
label: Blue Note
description: Live at the Lighthouse is a live album by jazz trumpeter Lee Morgan, released on the Blue Note label in 1971. The album features a quintet of Morgan, Bennie Maupin, Harold Mabern, Jymie Merritt, and Mickey Roker, recorded at The Lighthouse in Hermosa Beach, California in July 1970. Originally released as a double LP comprising four side-long recordings, the 1996 CD reissue expanded the track list with over one-hundred minutes of additional material from the Lighthouse gigs.
significance: Documents Morgan's evolution beyond soul jazz into more adventurous territory. Tragically, he would be killed two years later.

title: Miles Davis at Fillmore
label: Columbia
description: Miles Davis at Fillmore is a 1970 live album by the jazz trumpeter Miles Davis and band, recorded at the Fillmore East, New York City on four consecutive days, June 17 through June 20, 1970, originally released as a double vinyl LP. The performances featured the double keyboard set-up Davis toured with for a few months, with Keith Jarrett and Chick Corea playing electronic organ and Fender Rhodes electric piano, respectively. The group opened for Laura Nyro at these performances.
significance: Documented Miles's successful crossover to rock audiences. The band's energy and volume influenced both jazz and rock musicians.

title: Ptah, the El Daoud
label: Impulse!
description: Ptah, the El Daoud is the third solo album by American jazz pianist and harpist Alice Coltrane, released on Impulse! Records in September 1970. The album was recorded in the basement of her home in Dix Hills, New York, in a session on January 26, 1970.
significance: Showed Alice developing her own compositional voice. The combination of two contrasting tenor saxophonists creates a unique sound.

title: Red Clay
label: CTI
description: Red Clay is an album recorded in 1970 by jazz trumpeter Freddie Hubbard. It was his first album on Creed Taylor's CTI label and marked a shift toward the soul-jazz fusion sounds that would dominate his recordings in the later part of the decade. It entered at number 20 on Billboard’s Top 20 Best Selling Jazz LPs, on June 20, 1970.
significance: Bridged hard bop and fusion without losing artistic credibility. CTI's lush production was influential.

title: Superimposition
label: Tico
description: Superimposition is the twelfth studio album by American pianist Eddie Palmieri. It was released by Tico Records in 1970. The album combines modern salsa renditions of Cuban standards on side A with experimental descargas on side B. Since its release, the album has been praised by critics such as John Storm Roberts for its innovative approach.
significance: The album combines modern salsa renditions of Cuban standards on side A with experimental descargas on side B.
keyTracks: Que lindo eso, eh! / Isn't It Pretty

title: The Diverse Yusef Lateef
label: Atlantic
description: The Diverse Yusef Lateef is a jazz album by saxophonist Yusef Lateef released in 1970. In it are mixed influences from rhythm and blues and soul music and world music.
significance: In it are mixed influences from rhythm and blues and soul music and world music.

title: The Leon Thomas Album
label: Flying Dutchman
description: The Leon Thomas Album is the second album by American jazz vocalist and percussionist Leon Thomas recorded in 1970 and released by the Flying Dutchman label.
significance: A free jazz entry from 1970 by Leon Thomas, documenting the breadth of the free jazz period.

title: Transition
label: impulse!
description: Transition is an album of music by jazz saxophonist John Coltrane, recorded in 1965 but released posthumously only in 1970. As its title indicates, Transition was a bridge between classic quartet recordings like A Love Supreme and the more experimental works of Coltrane's last years.
significance: As its title indicates, Transition was a bridge between classic quartet recordings like A Love Supreme and the more experimental works of Coltrane's last years.
