title: Domino Theory
label: CBS/Sony
description: Domino Theory is the twelfth studio album by jazz fusion band Weather Report, released in April 1984. It is the second studio album to feature the Hakim-Bailey-Rossy rhythm section. The album contains seven tracks, three of which were recorded live.
significance: It is the second studio album to feature the Hakim-Bailey-Rossy rhythm section.

title: Palo pá rumba
label: Unknown
description: Palo pá rumba is a jazz fusion album by Eddie Palmieri from 1984. The date includes "Palo pá rumba" and "1983". The album comes from the contemporary era's stylistic eclecticism, drawing on the full history of jazz and beyond.
significance: He was the founder of the bands La Perfecta, La Perfecta II, and Harlem River Drive.

title: Straight Life - The Savoy Sessions
label: Savoy Jazz
description: Modern Art is an album by saxophonist Art Pepper featuring sessions recorded in late 1956 and early 1957 originally released on the Intro label. The album was reissued on CD on Blue Note Records with bonus tracks as Modern Art: The Complete Art Pepper Aladdin Recordings Volume 2 in 1988.
significance: Active primarily in West Coast jazz, Pepper first came to prominence in Stan Kenton's big band.

title: The Printmakers
label: Minor Music
description: The Printmakers is the first album by the pianist Geri Allen. It was recorded in February 1984 in Germany and released on the German Minor Music label.
significance: Introduced Allen as the leading pianist of her generation. Her synthesis of Cecil Taylor's energy and harmonic sophistication created a personal style.

title: Afro-Cuban Jazz
label: Unknown
description: Afro-Cuban jazz is the earliest form of Latin jazz. It mixes Afro-Cuban clave-based rhythms with jazz harmonies and techniques of improvisation. Afro-Cuban music has deep roots in African ritual and rhythm. The genre emerged in the early 1940s with the Cuban musicians Mario Bauzá and Frank Grillo "Machito" in the band Machito and his Afro-Cubans in New York City. In 1947, the collaborations of bebop trumpeter Dizzy Gillespie and percussionist Chano Pozo brought Afro-Cuban rhythms and instruments, such as the tumbadora and the bongo, into the East Coast jazz scene.
significance: The genre emerged in the early 1940s with the Cuban musicians Mario Bauzá and Frank Grillo "Machito" in the band Machito and his Afro-Cubans in New York City.

title: Black Codes (From the Underground)
label: Columbia
description: Black Codes (From the Underground) is a 1985 album by jazz trumpeter Wynton Marsalis.
significance: Announced the neo-traditional movement and proved that young musicians could engage with tradition without being trapped by it.

title: Cielo e terra
label: Direct Metal Mastering
description: Cielo e Terra is an album by jazz guitarist Al Di Meola that was released in 1985. Airto Moreira plays all percussion on "Traces ", "Cielo e Terra", "When You're Gone", and "Solace".
significance: Airto Moreira plays all percussion on "Traces ", "Cielo e Terra", "When You're Gone", and "Solace".

title: Hearts and Numbers
label: Hip Pocket Records
description: Out of the Loop is the seventh and final studio album by the Brecker Brothers that was released by GRP Records in 1994. In 1995 the album won the brothers two Grammy Awards for Best Contemporary Jazz Performance and Best Instrumental Composition.
significance: In 1995 the album won the brothers two Grammy Awards for Best Contemporary Jazz Performance and Best Instrumental Composition.

title: Sportin’ Life
label: Columbia
description: Sportin' Life is the thirteenth studio album by American jazz fusion band Weather Report which was released in June 1985 through Columbia Records. Although featuring many more vocal performances than any of their previous studio albums, words are rare and most vocals are chants from guest musicians such as Bobby McFerrin or Carl Anderson. The album is named after a character from Porgy and Bess. Sportin' Life was to be the last Weather Report studio album, but due to contractual obligations with Columbia the band had to release a follow-up, This Is This!, in 1986.
significance: Sportin' Life was to be the last Weather Report studio album, but due to contractual obligations with Columbia the band had to release a follow-up, This Is This!, in 1986.

title: The Third Decade
label: ECM
description: The Third Decade is an album by the Art Ensemble of Chicago recorded in June 1984 and released on ECM the following year.
significance: A ECM session that documents trumpeter Art Ensemble of Chicago working through the avant-garde jazz vocabulary of the contemporary period.

title: Tribal Tech
label: Passport
description: Tribal Tech is the fourth album by the jazz fusion band Tribal Tech, released in 1991.
significance: Influenced a generation of fusion guitarists with its technical demands and inventive compositions.

title: A música livre de Hermeto Paschoal
label: Fontana
description: Pianist Hermeto Pascoal's 1986 album A música livre de Hermeto Paschoal draws from the latin jazz tradition, released on Fontana. The album comes from the contemporary era's stylistic eclecticism, drawing on the full history of jazz and beyond.
significance: Pascoal was best known in Brazilian music for his orchestration and improvisation, as well as for being a record producer and contributor to many Brazilian and international albums.

title: Alma
label: Unknown
description: Alma is a 1986 album by Brazilian guitarist and composer Egberto Gismonti. Drawing on his dual mastery of guitar and piano, Gismonti weaves Brazilian folk melodies, classical forms, and jazz improvisation into a deeply personal statement. Tracks like "Baiao malandro" and "Loro" showcase his singular fingerpicking technique.
significance: From the contemporary era's stylistic eclecticism, drawing on the full history of jazz and beyond, this album positions Egberto Gismonti within the contemporary jazz conversation of the time.

title: Benny Carter All Stars
label: Sonet

significance: Standouts include "Easy Money" and "Memories of You".

title: Drum Night At Birdland
label: Vogue France
description: Drum Night At Birdland documents a 1986 studio date for Vogue France, with Art Blakey leading a tight hard bop ensemble. Among its key performances are 'Wee Dot' and 'Solo Philly Joe Jones'.
significance: Among its key performances are 'Wee Dot' and 'Solo Philly Joe Jones'.

title: Get Together
label: Pablo
description: Kansas City 8: Get Together is a small-group studio album by Count Basie, released on Pablo Records. Paring down from his famous big band, Basie leads an octet through a relaxed program of swing-rooted originals including "Ode to Pres" and "Basie's Bag," capturing the pianist's spare, economical style in an intimate setting.
significance: Pianist Count Basie's post-bop sensibility is on display in this 1986 session, a document of the contemporary era.

title: Legacy
label: Concord Jazz
description: Trumpeter Jon Faddis's 1986 album Legacy draws from the jazz fusion tradition, released on Concord Jazz. The date includes "West End Blues" and "Little Jazz".
significance: Upon his first appearance on the scene, he became known for his ability to closely mirror the sound of trumpet icon Dizzy Gillespie, who was his mentor along with pianist Stan Kenton and trumpeter Bill Catalano.

title: Royal Garden Blues
label: CBS
description: Royal Garden Blues is an album by the American saxophonist Branford Marsalis, released in 1986. Marsalis promoted it with a North American tour.

title: 'Round About Midnight at the Cafe Bohemia, Volume I

significance: For this reason, writer Gary Giddins said that Dorham's name has become "virtually synonymous with 'underrated'."

title: Civilization
label: Blue Note
description: Civilization is an album by the American musician Tony Williams, released in 1987. He supported it with a North American tour. The album peaked in the top five on Billboard's Jazz Albums chart.

label: Columbia

label: Columbia

The duo supported the album with a North American tour.

title: Satchmo
label: Pickwick Compact Discs
description: Louis Daniel Armstrong, nicknamed "Satchmo", "Satch", and "Pops", was an American jazz and blues trumpeter and vocalist. He was among the most influential figures in jazz. His career spanned five decades and several eras in the history of jazz. Armstrong received numerous accolades including the Grammy Award for Best Male Vocal Performance for Hello, Dolly! in 1965, as well as a posthumous win for the Grammy Lifetime Achievement Award in 1972.

The album comes from the contemporary era's stylistic eclecticism, drawing on the full history of jazz and beyond.

title: Look What I Got!
label: Verve
description: Look What I Got! is a 1988 album by vocalist Betty Carter, released on Verve. The record won Carter a Grammy Award for Best Jazz Vocal Performance, Female, capping a long career of uncompromising artistry. Her inventive phrasing and rhythmic daring are on full display across a program of standards and originals.
significance: Introduced Carter to a new generation after years of self-produced independence. Her uncompromising artistry finally received mainstream recognition.

title: Ritual: The Modern Jazz Messengers
label: Pacific Jazz
description: Ritual is a studio album by the Jazz Messengers featuring Art Blakey. Three years after being recorded, it was first released on the Pacific Jazz Records label as PJM-402, and reissued by them in 1962, with an Elmo Hope session, as Art Blakey & The Jazz Messengers/The Elmo Hope Quintet* Featuring Harold Land as PJ-33. It has been reissued on CD.
significance: Three years after being recorded, it was first released on the Pacific Jazz Records label as PJM-402, and reissued by them in 1962, with an Elmo Hope session, as Art Blakey & The Jazz Messengers/The Elmo Hope Quintet* Featuring Harold Land as PJ-33.

label: Enja Records

title: Stories
label: Unknown

title: The Eternal Triangle
label: Blue Note

It features performances by Hubbard, Shaw, Ray Drummond, Carl Allen, Mulgrew Miller and Kenny Garrett. The album followed the first Hubbard/Shaw collaboration Double Take (1985) and the two volumes were combined for the double CD release The Complete Freddie Hubbard and Woody Shaw Sessions (1995).
significance: The album followed the first Hubbard/Shaw collaboration Double Take (1985) and the two volumes were combined for the double CD release The Complete Freddie Hubbard and Woody Shaw Sessions (1995).

The music was arranged by Evans and members of his orchestra. The album was recorded in 1974 and performed by Evans with an orchestra featuring David Sanborn, Howard Johnson, Billy Harper, and John Abercrombie. The album was re-released with additional tracks on CD in 1988.

It features McLean in a sextet with trumpeter Webster Young, tuba player Ray Draper, pianist Gil Coggins, bassist George Tucker and drummer Larry Ritchie.

title: Jazz Street
label: Timeless Records
description: Red Rose Speedway is the second studio album by the British-American rock band Wings, although credited to "Paul McCartney and Wings". It was released through Apple Records in the United States on 30 April 1973 and in the United Kingdom on 4 May, preceded by its lead single, the ballad "My Love". The artist name change was made in the belief that the public's unfamiliarity with the band had been responsible for the weak commercial performance of the group's 1971 debut album Wild Life.
significance: The artist name change was made in the belief that the public's unfamiliarity with the band had been responsible for the weak commercial performance of the group's 1971 debut album Wild Life.

title: Ask the Ages

description: Sonny Sharrock's magnum opus, produced by Bill Laswell, features Pharoah Sanders, Elvin Jones, and Charnett Moffett in a supergroup that channels the fire of late-period Coltrane through Sharrock's savage, distortion-drenched guitar. The music is simultaneously spiritual and explosive.

title: In Private
label: Fresh Sound Records
description: "In Private" is a song by British singer Dusty Springfield, released as a single on 20 November 1989 by Parlophone and EMI. It was Springfield's third single in a row to be a chart success, after an absence of nearly two decades from the charts. Both "In Private" and Springfield's previous single, "Nothing Has Been Proved" were produced by Pet Shop Boys, who helped return Springfield to prominence with their 1987 hit collaboration "What Have I Done to Deserve This?". Both Springfield singles were included on her 1990 British album Reputation.
significance: It was Springfield's third single in a row to be a chart success, after an absence of nearly two decades from the charts.

significance: A pioneer of stride piano, he was one of the most important pianists in the early era of recording, and like Jelly Roll Morton, one of the key figures in the evolution of ragtime into what was eventually called jazz.

King, John Lee Hooker, Russell Malone, Wynton Marsalis and Linda Hopkins. It peaked at number 1 on the Top Jazz Albums chart. The album won the Grammy Award for Best Jazz Instrumental Performance, Individual or Group.

The record finds Osby pushing jazz-funk into adventurous territory, layering hip-hop rhythms and electronics beneath his angular saxophone lines. Tracks like "Mr. Gutterman" and "Raise" showcase the M-Base-influenced sound that distinguished Osby from his contemporaries.
significance: Released by Blue Note during the contemporary era's stylistic eclecticism, drawing on the full history of jazz and beyond, this set captures Greg Osby in characteristic form.

title: Blue Light 'Til Dawn
label: Blue Note
description: Blue Light 'til Dawn is a studio album by American jazz singer Cassandra Wilson. Her first album on the Blue Note label, it was released in 1993. It contains Wilson's interpretations of songs by various blues and rock artists, as well as three original compositions. The album marked a shift in Wilson’s recording style, mostly dropping the electric instruments of her earlier albums in favor of acoustic arrangements. A critical and commercial breakthrough, the album was re-released in 2014 with three bonus tracks recorded live somewhere in Europe during the Blue Light 'til Dawn Tour.
significance: Revitalized jazz vocals for a new generation. Wilson's approach—rooted but experimental—influenced countless singers.
keyTracks: You Don't Know What Love Is

title: Count Basie and His Great Vocalists

Despite a brief disbandment at the beginning of the 1950s, the band survived long past the big band era itself and the death of Basie in 1984. It continues under the direction of trumpeter Scotty Barnhart.

The set features stirring renditions of Masekela's best-known compositions, including the anti-apartheid anthem "Stimela" (The Coal Train) and the political rallying cry "Bring Him Back Home."
significance: Documented the euphoria of South Africa's liberation. Masekela had been an exile for three decades.

title: Just You, Just Me
label: Telarc Jazz
description: Just You, Just Me is a 1994 album by Dave Brubeck. This album is the 3rd of Brubeck's solo works preceded by Brubeck Plays Brubeck and Plays and Plays and.... There was a 37-year gap between "Brubeck Plays and Plays" and this album with Brubeck focusing on working with his quartet during that time. Brubeck writes that for this album, "...I prefer to record in the same way as I play at home..." with all of the songs on this album being first takes with no advanced editing. The exception to this is "I Understand" of which Brubeck did three takes playing each in different ways.
significance: Brubeck writes that for this album, "...I prefer to record in the same way as I play at home..." with all of the songs on this album being first takes with no advanced editing.

significance: Represented a new generation of post-apartheid South African jazz musicians who could freely draw on both indigenous traditions and international avant-garde. Ngqawana's vision was uniquely pan-African.

The record introduced Harris as one of the most exciting young voices on his instrument.

title: For Love or Country
label: Atlantic
description: Arturo Sandoval is a Cuban-American jazz trumpeter, pianist, timbalero, and composer. While living in his native Cuba, Sandoval was influenced by jazz musicians Charlie Parker, Clifford Brown, and Dizzy Gillespie. In 1977 he met Gillespie, who became his friend and mentor and helped him defect from Cuba while on tour with the United Nation Orchestra. Sandoval became an American naturalized citizen in 1998. His life was the subject of the film For Love or Country: The Arturo Sandoval Story (2000) starring Andy García.
significance: While living in his native Cuba, Sandoval was influenced by jazz musicians Charlie Parker, Clifford Brown, and Dizzy Gillespie.

title: Armstrong Alumni Allstars

description: Jazz is a music genre that originated in the African-American communities of New Orleans, Louisiana, in the late 19th and early 20th centuries. Its roots are in blues, ragtime, European harmony, African rhythmic rituals, spirituals, hymns, marches, vaudeville song, and dance music. Since the 1920s Jazz Age, it has been recognized as a major form of musical expression in traditional and popular music. Jazz is characterized by swing and blue notes, complex chords, call and response vocals, polyrhythms and improvisation.
significance: Since the 1920s Jazz Age, it has been recognized as a major form of musical expression in traditional and popular music.

His sophisticated use of harmony, including the use of tritone substitutions, his virtuosic solos exhibiting a departure from the dominant style of jazz trumpet innovator Louis Armstrong, and his strong impact on Dizzy Gillespie mark him as one of the most influential musicians of the swing era and a precursor of bebop.

title: Roi Boyé & The Gotham Minstrels
label: Sackville Recordings
description: Roi Boyé & the Gotham Minstrels is a solo album by Julius Hemphill. Billed as an "audiodrama," it was recorded at Matthew Studios in Larchmont, New York, during November 1976, and at Thunder Sound in Toronto during March 1977, and was released on vinyl by Sackville Records in 1977 as a two-LP set. In 2001, it was reissued on CD in limited quantities as part of the Sackville Collection series. On the album, Hemphill performs all instrumental parts via overdubbing, and is heard on alto saxophone, soprano saxophone, and flute.
significance: Billed as an "audiodrama," it was recorded at Matthew Studios in Larchmont, New York, during November 1976, and at Thunder Sound in Toronto during March 1977, and was released on vinyl by Sackville Records in 1977 as a two-LP set.

The set features Osby in a more reflective mode than his earlier M-Base recordings, with compositions like "Silent Attitude" and "Mentor's Praise" revealing a mature, searching approach to contemporary jazz.
significance: Features "Silent Attitude" and "Balloonman" in a contemporary jazz context, highlighting Greg Osby's interpretive range.

title: Soul of Things
label: ECM
description: Polish trumpeter Tomasz Stańko's haunting album with pianist Marcin Wasilewski's young trio is a masterclass in mood and space. Stańko's dark, fragile tone—shaped by decades of experience from free jazz to Krzysztof Komeda's film scores—hovers over the trio's luminous accompaniment.
significance: Introduced Stańko and the Wasilewski trio to international audiences and became one of ECM's best-selling albums of the 2000s. The music's Eastern European melancholy and beauty created a new standard for contemporary European jazz.

title: Swings

description: Pianist Art Tatum's 2002 album Swings draws from the swing tradition, released on High Definition Jazz. The date includes "I Know That You Know" and "Fifty Second Street Blues".
significance: Tatum also extended jazz piano's vocabulary and boundaries far beyond his initial stride influences, and established new ground through innovative use of reharmonization, voicing, and bitonality.

Well To The Bone is a 2002 album by fusion/jazz guitarist Scott Henderson. It is his third solo-album, again returning to his blues-roots.
It features a re-recording of the Tribal Tech song "Rituals".
significance: It is his third solo-album, again returning to his blues-roots.

title: Heartcore
label: Verve Music Group
description: Heartcore is Kurt Rosenwinkel's fifth album as a band leader. The album was fully produced by Rosenwinkel and Q-Tip of popular hip-hop group A Tribe Called Quest. The album is a departure musically from Rosenwinkel's previous work, as he contributes keyboard, drums, and voice, at times creating soundscapes completely on his own in his personal studio. Says Rosenwinkel, "There is a place, musically, that’s above the categories. This record – it’s jazz. And it’s much more".
significance: The album was fully produced by Rosenwinkel and Q-Tip of popular hip-hop group A Tribe Called Quest.

significance: Iyer received a 2013 MacArthur Fellowship, a Doris Duke Performing Artist Award, a United States Artists Fellowship, a Grammy nomination, and the Alpert Award in the Arts.
keyTracks: The Color of My Circumference I

title: Land of Giants
label: Telarc
description: Land of Giants is an album by McCoy Tyner released on the Telarc label in 2003. It was recorded in December 2002 and features performances of Tyner with vibraphonist Bobby Hutcherson, bassist Charnett Moffett and drummer Eric Harland. The Allmusic review by Matt Collar states that "While the work here is by no means as provocative as the stuff Hutcherson and Tyner produced in their heydays, it nonetheless proves them to be utter masters of the straight-ahead modern jazz idiom and should appeal to longtime fans".

title: Ballads
label: Music and Melody
description: Ballads is a jazz album by John Coltrane released in January 1963 by Impulse! Records. It was recorded in December 1961 and 1962, and released with catalogue number A-32 (mono) and AS-32 (stereo). Critic Gene Lees stated that the quartet had never played the tunes before. "They arrived with music-store sheet music of the songs" and just before the recordings, they "would discuss each tune, write out copies of the changes they'd use, semi-rehearse for a half hour and then do it". Each piece was recorded in one take, except for "All or Nothing at All".

description: Mingus Ah Um is a studio album by American jazz musician Charles Mingus which was released in October 1959 by Columbia Records. It was his first album recorded for Columbia. The title is a corruption of an imaginary Latin declension. It is common for Latin students to memorize Latin adjectives by first saying the masculine nominative, then the feminine nominative, and finally the neuter nominative singular —implying a transformation of his name, Mingus, Minga, Mingum. The album was inducted into the Grammy Hall of Fame in 2013. It was ranked 380 on the Rolling Stone's 500 Greatest Albums of All Time. The cover features a painting by S. Neil Fujita.

In the late 1960s he performed with Stan Getz and Miles Davis.
